Palladium.description: Palladium, with its lustrous white appearance, stands as a precious metal highly prized for its rarity and distinctive characteristics. Its notable usage in jewelry, especially as an alloy for crafting white gold.

Pallet.name: Pallet
Pallet.description: A standard bed made by carpenters for a good night's sleep usually consists of straw or hay covered with a sheet. This type of mattress is known as a palliasse or pallet, which is how it got its name.

Papaya.name: Papaya
Papaya.description: The ripe fruit of the papaya is usually eaten raw, without skin or seeds. The Papaya fruit is also used to tenderize meats as they provide a very specific taste. Herbalists provide the leaves to be smoked as it is believed that they help with breathing, or have the leaves turned into a paste as a laxative.

Paradiso.name: Paradiso
Paradiso.description: Paradiso is a rare and distinct granite color, found only in a few locations throughout the realms. It is in high demand but short supply, often causing regional prices to skyrocket. Its rich purple hue gives it a premium appearance, making it an exquisite choice for indoor furniture such as countertops and tables.

Pear.name: Pear
Pear.description: A common fruit cultivated all over. They are usually eaten fresh or stored as homemade juice. They are also used to create perry, a fermented pear juice, and a plethora of ciders.

Pearl Earrings.name: Pearl Earrings
Pearl Earrings.description: These earrings are typically made to order, using both common and exotic pearls such as freshwater, black, white, or silver. The cost of a commission vary wildly depending on the materials chosen. In some regions, they serve a ceremonial purpose, worn by senior clergy as part of their sacred vestments.

Peas.name: Peas
Peas.description: Garden peas are often eaten boiled and flavored with butter or spearmint as a side dish vegetable. To preserve peas, drying has been used for generations and the product is mainly used in pea soups. Fresh green peas can also be stir-fried or depending on the region, put into dumplings.

Petalite.name: Petalite
Petalite.description: These ornamental stones are moderately hard yet brittle, with colors ranging from pink to colorless. A curious event with Petalite occurs when a jeweler cuts the polished gem, the smallest piece momentarily loses all reflections. This effect has sparked speculation of arcane origins, though it may simply be an optical illusion. Unfortunately, a significant quantity of Petalite from a consistent source remains elusive for rigorous arcane investigation.

Pewter.name: Pewter
Pewter.description: Pewter, a tin-based alloy that includes copper, antimony, and sometimes lead, is a popular material among blacksmiths for crafting tableware such as plates, cups, and utensils but also for decorative objects, mainly collectible statuettes and figurines, game figures, ship and other models, (replica) coins, pendants, plated jewellery and so on.

Piano.name: Piano
Piano.description: The piano is a keyboard instrument that generates sound by striking strings with wooden hammers when its keys are pressed. Its production is quite expensive due to the need for skilled craftsmanship, involving experienced carpenters, skilled blacksmiths, and adept machinists. The piano plays a significant role in various musical genres, serving as both a solo instrument and an essential component in orchestras and ensembles for high-class citizens.

Pickaxe.name: Pickaxe
Pickaxe.description: Pickaxes, also referred to as mattocks, are tools used for breaking up rocks, paving, or earth, commonly used in activities such as digging, soil preparation, demolition, or specialized applications like mining.

Pickled Olives.name: Pickled Olives
Pickled Olives.description: Pickled Olives can be prepared whole or stuffed with garlic cloves or red peppers. They are versatile enough to be included in salads, enjoyed as a standalone snack, or even used in various alcoholic beverages.

Pig Iron.name: Pig Iron
Pig Iron.description: Pig Iron, commonly referred to as crude iron, is an intermediate product used by the blacksmiths in the production of steel. When combined with charcoal, through careful refining, impurities are removed, and the resulting steel becomes an amazing material for tools, construction, and warfare.

Pike.description: Pikes, solitary hunters, lie motionless in the water or lurk within dense clusters of aquatic weeds. When prey comes within reach, they strike with a sudden, rapid lunge, seizing their target with razor-sharp teeth. They are known to snatch not just fish but also waterfowl and even small farm animals. The pike is a fearsome predator and is as relentless as they are voracious. With their large mouths and insatiable appetites, pike can grow to astonishing sizes, dwarfing most other freshwater fish.
Pike.name: Pike

Pillory.description: The pillory was a device used for publicly punishing repeat offenders. It features a wooden frame with holes to secure the head and hands.
Pillory.name: Pillory

Pistachio Pie.name: Pistachio Pie
Pistachio Pie.description: Made with cream cheese, heavy cream, and plenty of pistachios, this pie is one of the softest. Since most recipes require no cooking, it is a constant item in stock for both bakeries and taverns.

Pitchfork.name: Pitchfork
Pitchfork.description: The pitchfork, commonly referred to as a hay fork, is a tool crafted for pitching loose materials, including hay, straw, manure, or leaves. With its long handle and typically two to five thin tines, it is specifically designed for the efficient movement of such materials.

Platinum Ingot.name: Platinum Ingot
Platinum Ingot.description: Platinum Ingots are extremely difficult to manufacture, requiring the hottest smelters fueled by lava or arcane fire to properly melt and refine platinum ore. Once purified into ingot form, only the most skilled craftsmen and elite workshops use it for the creation of high-quality items. However, the ingots are traded mainly as a commodity for high-value goods.

Platinum Ore.description: Platinum Ore is an extremely rare metal, found exclusively through underground mining. It possesses remarkable resistance to corrosion, even under high-temperature smelting, making it one of the most noble metals. Platinum is primarily used to create alloys with gold by jewelers for making intricate rings and necklaces sold at a quite high premium price.
Platinum Ore.name: Platinum Ore

Plum.name: Plum
Plum.description: An oval fleshy fruit that is purple, reddish, or yellow, in color, when ripe and contains a flattish pointed stone. They are considered one of the juiciest fruits across the realms. They are used mainly by bakers to create a variety of sweets from plum tarts to almond cakes. Some tavern keeps use them to make a strong alcohol with a very powerful flavor.

Plum Seeds.name: Plum Seeds
Plum Seeds.description: The process of planting plum trees shares many similarities with that of peaches. However, it requires even more attention to temperature control. The seeds need a period of cold exposure to break dormancy, and the timing must be just right to ensure that the seeds are not exposed to too much warmth or freezing temperatures. This critical period is why only the most experienced farmhands or herbalists take on the task, ensuring that the plum seeds have the ideal conditions for successful seeding.

Pomegranate.name: Pomegranate
Pomegranate.description: Pomegranate is a sweet, tart fruit with thick, red skin. While the skin is not edible, it holds hundreds of juicy seeds that you can eat plain or sprinkle on salads, oatmeal, and other dishes.

Poplar Log.name: Poplar Log
Poplar Log.description: Poplar is one of the most available types of wood. Due to its lower price due to its accessibility, it is an excellent choice for most beginners to intermediate carpenters. It also has a wide range of uses, such as construction, joinery, or carving.

Pouch.description: The simple is mainly made from simple cloth, sturdy leather, or fine silk and features a drawstring that threads through reinforced holes in a looping pattern. It is mainly intended to carry coins, though it can also hold materials or small objects depending on the need.
Pouch.name: Pouch

Prehnite.name: Prehnite
Prehnite.description: Prehnite is often found in the lining of gas cavities in hardened lava formations. However, the material rarely possesses the required thickness or attractive coloration to be considered for gemstone cutting.

Pressure Gauge.name: Pressure Gauge
Pressure Gauge.description: An pressure gauge is a measuring instrument used to monitor the force within contained systems, such as steam conduits or alchemical apparatus. Some variants are focused on arcane mechanisms, using enchanted fluids or elemental spirits to detect fluctuations in force that would be invisible to the naked eye.

Pressurized Container.name: Pressurized Container
Pressurized Container.description: Created specifically to contain the most volatile materials and keep them under pressure, such as specific gases, air, breaths of mythical beasts, or arcane compounds. Usually, if one of these fissures occurs, even though made from sturdy materials, disaster strikes.

Pumpkin.name: Pumpkin
Pumpkin.description: It is assumed to be one of the most festive vegetables out there. Pumpkins are used in most festival events for Pumpkin carving, decorations or pumpkin chunking in which teams attempt to throw pumpkins as far as possible with the help of catapults, trebuchets or ballistas are often common in these games. Pumpkins sometimes are also associated with local legends where the pumpkin is a stand-in for a creature's head. Taverns keep the seeds and roast them to provide to their guests as a snack, and with the help of the pulp, one of the most delicious forms of pie is created, however, it can be an acquired taste.

Quark Balls.name: Quark Balls
Quark Balls.description: Quark balls are a type of pastry made from a mixture of cheese, eggs, flour, sugar, lemon zest, and vanilla extract and after cooking, sprinkled with cinnamon and sugar. These baked goods are mostly served at major events, like festivals, season markets, as well as, carnivals.

Quartzite.name: Quartzite
Quartzite.description: Quartzite is a decorative stone and may be used to cover walls, as roofing tiles, flooring, and stairsteps, due to its strength, it is also used to construct roads, bridges, and dams. When cut and polished, the rock is quite beautiful, as well as durable.

Quince.description: A common fruit in most home gardens, it is often preserved over the coldest of winters with little care. Quince can also be used for hay fever, stomach pains, and many other conditions, however, it is mainly known for its use in jellies and jams.
Quince.name: Quince

Ravenar.name: Ravenar
Ravenar.description: The luster of this gem closely resembles the eyes of a raven, and those who are well-versed with magic casting speculate that it has the ability to hold the souls of the living. It is strongly believed that it possess a dark energy, to help with with the arts of the undead, and its smooth, glossy surface seems to pulse similarity to a slow heartbeat

Records.name: Records
Records.description: Public records are typically created by the local archivist to store information specific to the town. This information is kept to track changes over extended periods of time. The records usually include details such as buildings constructed, resources harvested and traded, taxes collected, elections held, and many other town-specific matters. The archivist ensures that the public records are well-organized, accessible, and preserved for future generations.

Red Cabbage.name: Red Cabbage
Red Cabbage.description: The red cabbage is often used raw for salads and coleslaw, a perfect side dish for roast goose or turkey. The red cabbage is a farmer's favorite as it lasts winters, as is, not needing to be turned into sauerkraut in order to be preserved.

Red Cherry Sticks.name: Red Cherry Sticks
Red Cherry Sticks.description: Cherry sticks are a popular candy made from simple ingredients like sugar, starch, wheat flour, gelatin, and cherries. The combination of the chewy outer layer with the smooth, sweet middle creates a delightful treat for any young commoner.

Red Dye.name: Red Dye
Red Dye.description: The red dye is relatively rare and is primarily found in specific insects that are mostly located on cacti. Depending on the climate and territory, they may not be easily accessible. After harvesting, the insects are crushed into a fine powder, dried, and filtered to remove any residue. The remaining material can be used as a dye for fabrics or paints.

Red Obsidian.name: Red Obsidian
Red Obsidian.description: Red Obsidian is also referred to as Mahogany Obsidian, due to the color of the gem resembling the wood. This is formed when the arcane arts cool pools of lava quickly. Some legerdemains try to sell these as a premium under the premise that it has untapped magical potential, however, their usual price is close to the one of the regular obsidian.

Red Onion.name: Red Onion
Red Onion.description: Also referred to as purple onion, it is usually served fresh, as a salad or part of mixed salads, and especially as a compulsory garnish for some traditional bean-and-smoked ham soups.

Red Topaz.name: Red Topaz
Red Topaz.description: Also referred to as Jacinth, Flamegem, or sometimes Corundum it is one of the most common gems used in magical crafting. Due to its very specific use, its price can range widely. Outside of magical use it is one of the most durable minerals after the Diamond. Gaffers use it as a cheaper alternative to create glass cutters.

Redwood Log.name: Redwood Log
Redwood Log.description: A universal wood log, it can be used in anything really but usually found in carpentry and general construction, ranging from indoor and outdoor furniture, shingles, fence posts, paneling, and even arrow shafts.

Redwood Plank.name: Redwood Plank
Redwood Plank.description: Usually used for creating boxes, tables, stools, bats, and bridges, and due to it, it has quite a high demand for anyone with a background in any form of construction.

Reed Plant.name: Reed Plant
Reed Plant.description: The common water reed occurs along the margins of lakes, fens, marshes, and streams, from the most Nordic regions to the warmest places. Dried reed stems have been used for aeons as thatching and construction material, in basketry, for arrows and pens, and in musical instruments.

Regal Mark.name: Regal Mark
Regal Mark.description: When a king ascends to the throne, whether by succession, conquest, or assassination, a unique medallion coin is forged for them. This gold medallion features a dark-red diamond at its center, serving as a commemoration of the event and a means of quick identification if the king ever needs to blend in with their subjects. As each medallion is unique and only a limited number exist in circulation, some have the buying power to purchase anything on an entire continent.

Rhenium.description: Rhenium, sometimes referred to as the "Alchemist's Metal," is primarily used in the making of alloys with tungsten. To experienced alchemists, Rhenium symbolizes the perfection of all matter physical, mental, and spiritual. In many potions, a single drop of liquid Rhenium is added to strengthen their potency.
Rhenium.name: Rhenium

Rhodizite.name: Rhodizite
Rhodizite.description: This precious stone, with its translucent appearance ranging from pale yellow to green, was commonly found in small, fragmented crystals. Due to its limited cleavage, it was primarily utilized for carving rather than faceting. Gem cutters focused their efforts on crystals that could be fashioned into faceted gems.

Rhodochrosite.name: Rhodochrosite
Rhodochrosite.description: Or commonly named Rosenstone is highly demanded by nobility due to its pink color. It's well known that they usually go out of their way to acquire this type of gem. Used for ornamentation in earrings, rings, necklaces, or bracelets. Several Rosenstone pieces, if correctly crushed and mixed with additional herbs, are assumed to neutralize most poisons and diseases.

Rhodonite.name: Rhodonite
Rhodonite.description: Rhodonite is an attractive gemstone primarily valued for its ornamental use, featuring vivid pink coloring. It is frequently incorporated into jewelry, typically in the form of beads or cabochons.

Rhubarb.name: Rhubarb
Rhubarb.description: One of the rarest spring fruits, usually used in pies and tarts, or sometimes used to create sugars. Herbalists often prescribe it as a remedy for issues related to indigestion, or to calm the nerves when strong emotions are involved. In magic, it is used, on rare occasions, as a spell component.

Rhubarb Pie.name: Rhubarb Pie
Rhubarb Pie.description: Rhubarb Pie delights with its sweet and tangy taste, accentuated by a fruity filling that holds together nicely. Since rhubarb is a seasonal vegetable, this pie is only available for a limited time after the cold seasons.

Rock Salt.name: Rock Salt
Rock Salt.description: Salt is used extensively in cooking as a key ingredient, and to cure a wide variety of foods such as pork and fish. Gatherers create a mixture of salt and water to use as a weed killer in fields of valuable crops.

Rockfish.name: Rockfish
Rockfish.description: Rockfish, also known as Rock Cod or Rock Snapper, inhabit rocky reefs and seafloors, ranging from shallow nearshore waters to the deep ocean. Beneath the ocean’s surface, Rockfish hover near rocky outcrops, their spiny dorsal fins blending well into the rugged terrain. Some Rockfish dwell in the water column, while others remain motionless beneath the kelp canopy in the giant kelp forests.

Rose.name: Rose
Rose.description: Rose is a flower with many purposes for the regular folk. Symbolically, it serves as a timeless token of love, often shared on first dates or anniversaries. Practically, rose bushes are used as a gardening tool to enhance the visual appeal of parks and gardens. In some distinct regions, rose jam is coveted for its distinctive flavor.

Rose Gold.name: Rose Gold
Rose Gold.description: Rose gold, created by combining pure gold and copper, is popular in specialized jewelry. It is more malleable than pure gold, making it a preferred material for whitesmiths, and it is less expensive than gold or white gold.

Runner Peanut.name: Runner Peanut
Runner Peanut.description: Peanuts are useful in the making of peanut oil, peanut butter, roasted peanuts, salted and sweet peanut bars, and peanut sauce but they can also be eaten raw or salted.

Rutabaga.name: Rutabaga
Rutabaga.description: Rutabaga is often seen as a food of last resort, linked to food shortages in wartime. Boiled stew made solely from rutabaga and water is a common dish during major conflicts. Rutabaga roots and tops serve as a valuable forage crop for winter livestock feed.

Rutile.name: Rutile
Rutile.description: Rutile, in its natural crystal state, serves two main distinct purposes, decorating pottery with intricate designs and adding ornamentation to already placed gemstones. Found in large deposits, it is sometimes used in crafting entire pottery pieces or as striking ornamental accents on furniture.

Safe Balm.name: Safe Balm
Safe Balm.description: A small balm created by alchemists to alleviate pain. It is however speculated that, if it is rubbed on the skin, it can also cure fevers, as well, as colds. This is a belief held by most commonfolk and usually used to treat their children.

Sailing Mast.name: Sailing Mast
Sailing Mast.description: The sailing mast is a pole that rises vertically from a ship and supports the sails. Larger sailboats often have more than one mast. The mast plays a crucial role in supporting the sails, allowing the wind to propel the ship.

Salmon Steak.name: Salmon Steak
Salmon Steak.description: Salmon, primarily sourced from specific cold regions, is considered a rare and pricey meal due to its scarcity. Taverns, often with divine or magical assistance to keep it fresh, obtain salmon and typically sell it at a substantial premium pricing.

Salty Cocoa Nut Bars.description: Salty cocoa nut bars are a type of snack bar that typically consists of a mixture of nuts, seeds, and dried fruits that are bound together with a sweet and salty mixture of cocoa, honey or maple syrup, and a pinch of salt. These may not have much appeal to younger folk, but after a long day at work and heading towards the inn, a commoner might choose to have a couple of these as a replacement meal.
Salty Cocoa Nut Bars.name: Salty Cocoa Nut Bars

Samarskite.name: Samarskite
Samarskite.description: Samarskite, a scarce semi-precious gemstone, is sought after despite its lower price. It is rarely worn and traditionally reserved for ceremonies honoring the loss of a loved one.

Samite.name: Samite
Samite.description: One of the most luxurious and heavy silks throughout the realms, mainly used in ecclesiastical robes, vestments, ornamental fabrics, and interior decoration. For those of royal blood it is assumed that a combination of cloth made out of gold and samite is used for their clothing.

Samson Fish.name: Samson Fish
Samson Fish.description: Samson Fish, rarely referred to as the Kingfish due to its large size, is not considered ideal for cooking as it often contains various parasites. Instead, it is primarily sought after as a trophy catch in many sports fishing competitions rather than for consumption.

Sandalwood Log.name: Sandalwood Log
Sandalwood Log.description: Sandalwood is primarily harvested for its oil. The Sandalwood forests are considered one of the most expensive woods within the realms. From the oil, a lot can be crafted such as perfumes, soaps, candles, and incense, as well as, if turned into a fine powder, for medicine, religious or cultural purposes, also, as a flavor for foods and beverages.

Sandalwood Oil.name: Sandalwood Oil
Sandalwood Oil.description: Harvested from the Sandalwood tree it is mainly used by herbalists to create poultices, mainly for skin irritation and diseases, however, the oil can be also used in cooking to add a mild sandalwood flavor to most recipes.

Sandwich.name: Sandwich
Sandwich.description:
"Rumor has it that the knowledge of making sandwiches has disappeared over the years. Recently, a traveler named Peach mentioned that my compendium lacked any sandwich recipes, and I felt a bit bad for this oversight, particularly since sandwiches are so well-known. Nevertheless, I believe I possess notes from an old weary traveler on how to prepare one."

A sandwich is a food item made of two pieces of bread with a filling in between, usually consumed as a light meal. The filling can be anything the creator chooses, ranging from eggs and bacon to jams and cooked beans.

Sardonyx.name: Sardonyx
Sardonyx.description: A form of agate, higher in price, usually only in a combination of two colors with black bands across. Wearing this specific gemstone as an adornment is assumed to ward off a spell, however, the details of it were lost to time.

Satin Spar.name: Satin Spar
Satin Spar.description: Satin Spar, a fibrous variety of gypsum, is distinguished by its pearly luster, sparkling sheen, and silky texture. Warriors heading into battle frequently wear these stones, believing their luster can blind far-away archers and shield them from projectiles.

Scallions.description: Scallions, also known as green onions in some regions, are prized for their mild flavor, which distinguishes them from other types of onions. They are prevalent in salads and various culinary dishes.
Scallions.name: Scallions

Scallop.name: Scallop
Scallop.description: Scallops are primarily found in shallow waters among seagrasses on sandy or muddy seabeds, often near deep estuaries, tidal rivers, and harbors, making them an excellent target for novice fishermen. They are primarily harvested for food and are often breaded, deep-fried, and served with coleslaw, potato fries, or mashed potatoes.

Scarlet.name: Scarlet
Scarlet.description: The most expensive woolen cloth material, often dyed with kermes dye, created from rare insects, which provides a bright red color to the fabric making it one the most expensive dyes in use. This type of material is only used by the finest seamstresses or dressmakers.

Scheelite.name: Scheelite
Scheelite.description: Scheelite gemstones are found in shades ranging from pale yellow to orange and are suitable for faceting, slicing, and polishing, making them ideal for inlay work. Larger, irregular specimens are commonly mounted in silver as pendants or used for furniture ornamentation.

Schematics.name: Schematics
Schematics.description: The schematics appear to be old, though most of the details remain, albeit faded. First, a historian is needed to date the pages, approximate what materials were used, and assess their origin. To fully restore the missing information, a master of their craft is required, as only they would possess the expertise necessary for a complete restoration.

Scorpionfish.name: Scorpionfish
Scorpionfish.description: Scorpionfish have extremely potent venom in their sharp spines, making them one of the most poisonous creatures in the ocean. However, despite their toxicity, they are mostly considered a delicacy, often praised as one of the most delicious fish available. Proper preparation is crucial, as incorrectly handling the fish can lead to severe poisoning or even fatalities. Herbalists frequently place direct orders with fishermen to harvest the venom for medicinal and alchemical purposes.

Scythe.name: Scythe
Scythe.description: The scythe is a tool predominantly utilized by farmers for mowing grass, grain, or other crops, the scythe consists of a long, curving blade attached at an angle to an extended handle. In certain regions, it carries an association with death, a belief particularly held by the most superstitious commoners.

Sea Daric.name: Sea Daric
Sea Daric.description: A gleaming gold disc, emblazoned with a highly detailed carved skull. These coins are often crafted from pillaged gold obtained from various sources such as gold coins, jewelry, and gold bars. The weight of the coin itself is heavy, serving as a tangible reminder of the riches and dangers of the high seas. Throughout the minting process, the crafter ensures that the gold is as close to 100% pure as possible, making it one of the most valuable coins in circulation.

Sea Salt.name: Sea Salt
Sea Salt.description: Sea salt, often known as bay salt, is harvested by evaporating seawater, either through boiling or by allowing the sun to do the work in specialized coastal salt works, known as salterns. This mineral is essential in cooking, food preservation, alchemy, leather tanning, and countless other uses.

Seeds - Pumpkin.name: Seeds - Pumpkin
Seeds - Pumpkin.description: Pumpkin seeds are small, hard seeds that come from pumpkins. They can be roasted and eaten as a snack or used to make oil and other products. Pumpkin seeds might be imbued with the power to cure illnesses, making them a valuable commodity for herbalists and those seeking to improve their health.

Seleneium.name: Seleneium
Seleneium.description: Selenium is a rare and precious mineral that is highly sought after for its unique properties. It is said to have a deep, iridescent luster that shimmers with every color of the rainbow. When it is struck with a hammer or chisel, it gives off a pure, clear tone that is said to have a soothing effect on those who hear it. Despite its many wondrous properties, Selenium is also said to be incredibly difficult to obtain, and many brave and daring adventurers have set out in search of it, only to be met with failure or their demise.

Serpentine.name: Serpentine
Serpentine.description: Serpentine, sometimes referred to as "Poor man's Jade", is a relatively common gemstone. It is typically cut into round or oval shapes known as cabochons, which are then used to adorn weapons, armor, or jewelry.

Sewing Needle.name: Sewing Needle
Sewing Needle.description: A sewing needle, utilized for hand-sewing and crafted from steel or bone, is a long slender tool with a pointed tip at one end and a hole, or eye, designed to hold the sewing thread.

Sextant.name: Sextant
Sextant.description: The sextant is a navigation instrument designed to measure the angular distance between two visible objects. Primarily, it is used to measure the angle between an astronomical body, such as a star or a moon, and the horizon, which is crucial for nautical navigation.

Shallot.name: Shallot
Shallot.description: Shallots are widely used in cooking and can be pickled as well. Thinly sliced and deep-fried shallots are commonly used as a condiment in cuisine, especially with porridge. They offer a flavor akin to onions but with a milder profile. Like onions, cutting raw shallots releases compounds that irritate the eyes and cause tears. Some herbalists collect shallots for making poultices, harnessing the shallots and their tear-inducing qualities.

Shark Teeth.name: Shark Teeth
Shark Teeth.description: Shark teeth are very popular to trade, collect, and sell, making them a very valuable commodity. Depending on the region the shark's teeth are used as weapons or spearheads. They are also used as tools that help to cut food, carve wood, or dig with. They are also used to make jewelry, mainly necklaces and bracelets.

Shorthorn Beef.name: Shorthorn Beef
Shorthorn Beef.description: This particular beef slice is known by many names, and those with a refined taste often delve into tracing the meat's location and the bovine's genealogical tree to sell it at a premium.

Shovel.name: Shovel
Shovel.description: The Shovel is a tool used to dig as well as to move loose dirt, gravel, grain, or snow, from one spot to another. It can also be used for making trenches and uprooting deep-rooted weeds and plants.

Siltstone.name: Siltstone
Siltstone.description: Siltstone, a type of mudrock, is made of compressed clay and silt. It absorbs water well, making it useful for aquifers and water storage but unsuitable for most construction due to its porous nature, and when it is, it is primarily used in areas where no other type of rock material is available.

Silver Blue Spinel Ring.name: Silver Blue Spinel Ring
Silver Blue Spinel Ring.description: Dark blue spinel, a beautiful gem, is cut by skilled gemcutters and embedded into a silver or iron ring. Even though it is set in more affordable metals, the spinel itself elevates the ring's value to a level comparable with those made of more precious materials. Its rich hue of the gem, makes the ring highly desirable by nobility.

Silver Winter.name: Silver Winter
Silver Winter.description: This coin is a symbol of achievement and status within the bardic colleges. After successfully completing their last year of college, attendees are given a copper coin crafted by art students, featuring intricate seasonal markings. The coin is also available in other patterns, such as Silver Solar, Snow, or Summer, each corresponding to a different season. Despite its name, the coin is made of copper and holds a value similar to that of a silver piece.

Skipjack Tuna.name: Skipjack Tuna
Skipjack Tuna.description: Skipjack are the smallest and most abundant of the tuna species. For bait, live fish such as herring, mackerel, or squid are preferred. Their streamlined, mostly scale-free bodies make them ideal for long-term preservation through salting, smoking, or pickling.

Skull Candle.name: Skull Candle
Skull Candle.description: Tavern-keeps and innholders are their primary patrons. They are cheap to buy and cheaper to maintain, and they give a place a certain... character. I also must say, they are particularly fond of them, claiming the grim visage keeps rowdy dockhands in their seats. I find they are more often used as a target for throwing knives after the seventh round of ale, but for one night a year, I think the keeps can manage.

Skull Ring.name: Skull Ring
Skull Ring.description: While a staple for the Red Harvest, wear one long enough and folk assume you’ve done something grim, as I forgot to take it of for a few. I wore one for a fortnight on the in a runty village while traveling, just to see, and the dockhands and farmers alike, gave me a wide berth, muttering that I must’ve lost a crew to the deep, or that something horrible has happened to me.

Sky Blue.name: Sky Blue
Sky Blue.description: The small mushroom is a distinctive all-blue color, in some places called Werewere-kokako, due to its similar coloring to the Kokako bird. Its beauty is deceptive, for its poison brings swift death to those who dare to eat it. For the skilled artisan, it offers something different, a blue dye so rich and rare to be used for ordinary cloth to transform it into a work of art.

Sled.name: Sled
Sled.description: Sleds are designed in various shapes and sizes to accommodate various scenarios. They range from children's sleds, perfect for racing down large hills, to sturdy traveling sleds used by sled dogs to traverse vast winter landscapes.

Small Cabbage Sprouts.name: Small Cabbage Sprouts
Small Cabbage Sprouts.description: Different regions have their own names for small cabbage sprouts, usually based on where they are found. Chefs in small restaurants and cooks in local taverns often make a special effort to find these sprouts. Because they are somewhat rare, dishes made with them are typically way more expensive. Some good examples include mixed cabbage salads or caramelized cabbage sprouts.

Smith's Coin.name: Smith's Coin
Smith's Coin.description: When a customer places a custom order or commission at a specialty shop, such as a jewelry store or blacksmith, a coin bearing the shop's symbol is provided. The customer's name is etched on the back of the coin, and they are asked to sign a ledger. Once the item has been crafted or procured, the owner can retrieve it by trading in the coin. The value of the coin is equivalent to the cost of the commissioned item. If the coin is lost or stolen, it becomes worthless, and only the rightful owner can retrieve the item using the original coin.

Smithsonite.name: Smithsonite
Smithsonite.description: Smithsonite, also referred to as zinc spar, is frequently discovered in zinc ore deposits. In earlier eras, it was believed to possess the remarkable ability to restore sight to the blind, though the ancient methods for achieving this feat have faded into obscurity. Modern alchemists persist in their quest to uncover and revive these lost arts.

Smoked Meats.name: Smoked Meats
Smoked Meats.description: The most common method of smoking meats involves using hardwoods like oak, hickory, cherry, or acacia, either individually or in combination. These woods impart distinct flavors to the meat while also aiding in its preservation. Inside the smokehouse, the meat is hung on hooks above a drip tray. Common smoked products include bacon, fish, ham, sausages, salami, and pastrami, each offering a unique taste and flavor.

Snapping Death Worm.description: Snapping Death Worm, or simply called Bobbit, is an subterranean critter that burrows mostly on beaches, however, every recolection of him resembles a horror story. It senses vibrations on the surface to ambush prey, launching itself upward to drag creatures underground with its snapping jaws.
Snapping Death Worm.name: Snapping Death Worm

Soot.name: Soot
Soot.description: Deep black powder or flaky substance, produced by burning material. Used by alchemists and herbalists as a binding agent, and by the weavers as dyes for a plethora of textiles.

Sour Cherry.name: Sour Cherry
Sour Cherry.description: Directly harvested from the tree, Sour Cherries can be delicious when spooned into one's mouth. However, some may find it an acquired taste. The concentrated and juicy result is perfect for pairing with alcoholic beverages, custard, pies, or pudding.

Spicebush.description: The Northern Lindera, infamous as an invasive species, is swiftly dealt with by herbalists who seek to protect local ecosystems. Yet, its fruit finds its way into kitchens, where it is appreciated as a flavorful seasoning for meats, soups, and hearty dishes, to the discontent of the herbalists.
Spicebush.name: Spicebush

Spider Beetle.name: Spider Beetle
Spider Beetle.description: While the Spider Beetle is harmless to people, spotting it near granaries are problematic. They feed on the dried food supplies, particularly grains and fruits, seeing it, usually, it shows that stored provisions have gone bad.

Spider Silk.name: Spider Silk
Spider Silk.description: Spider silk is highly valued and expensive, despite being a relatively abundant resource. Few people attempt to gather it due to the challenges involved. Although it sells for a high price, silk from larger spiders is usually a bit less costly. The harvesting process involves stunning the spiders and using a specialized extractor to collect the silk.

Spinach.name: Spinach
Spinach.description: Its leaves are a common edible vegetable consumed fresh, stir-fried, or steamed. Herbalists use wine fortified with spinach juice to give to the injured with the intent of reducing or stopping bleeding.

Spindle.name: Spindle
Spindle.description: Commonly made from either wood, bone or metal, the spindle is a rod for spinning wool, silk, and other natural fibers. Among weavers and tailors, it stands out as one of their most cherished tools.

Spring Water.name: Spring Water
Spring Water.description: Spring water is collected from underground springs and is mainly used by alchemists. It is highly sought after, and with the help of magical casters, the water is purified even further as it is generally used to create potions. Magical water streams are rare yet when found it is speculated that the potion created using this specific type of water highly increases its potency.

Squash.name: Squash
Squash.description: Gourds, Pumpkins, and Zucchini are types of squashes, and they are used in multiple culinary recipes, squash is often roasted, boiled, or steamed. It is usually served with butter or olive oil and savory seasonings, it also counts as an ingredient for sweet bread.

Stitch-me-up Pie.name: Stitch-me-up Pie
Stitch-me-up Pie.description: These pies show up most often in autumn markets, when bakers want to flaunt their skill as much as their orchard’s bounty. The more intricate the “stitching,” the higher the price. In my travels, a while back, I once saw a baker take commissions from adventuring companies, shaping pies with gashes that matched their battle scars. Silly? Absolutely. But the guildsmen paid handsomely, and everyone left sticky-fingered and grinning.

Stone Brick.name: Stone Brick
Stone Brick.description: The stone brick is often expensive to produce, as it requires specialized tools and skilled craftsmen or masons to shape and polish, as well, and must be mined from deep within the earth or quarried from rock formations, making it often reserved for the most prestigious building projects, such as castles, walls, forts, and fortresses.

Stones of Mother Earth.name: Stones of Mother Earth
Stones of Mother Earth.description: These earrings are made with diopside, and their name is taken from the reverence druids hold for this particular stone in high regard. The beautiful light green gem, set in gold, creates an allure of elegance and an air of nobility. The price reflects this appearance, aligning with the refined impression they leave.

Stopwatch.name: Stopwatch
Stopwatch.description: Mechanical stopwatches are powered by a mainspring, which must be wound up, usually by turning the knurled knob at the top of the stopwatch. They are primarily used to measure elapsed time in various fields such as sports, training, and science for activities like running laps or conducting experiments.

Strawberry.name: Strawberry
Strawberry.description: Strawberries are a favorite fruit among all folk as it is believed they bring happiness. In addition to being consumed fresh, strawberries can be made into jam, as well as dried to be used in cooked foods.

Sugar Pumpkin.name: Sugar Pumpkin
Sugar Pumpkin.description: Sugar pumpkins are a type of winter squash recognized for their sweet flavor and firm, smooth, dense flesh, making them perfect for pumpkin pies and other baked goods like cookies and loaves of bread.

Sugarcane.name: Sugarcane
Sugarcane.description: Sugarcane, predominantly grown in tropical regions, is primarily cultivated for sugar production. Sugar mills extract raw sugar from freshly harvested cane, and 'mill-white' sugar is occasionally produced right after the initial extraction phase at these mills, often for local markets.

Sunberry.name: Sunberry
Sunberry.description: Or Wonderberries sometimes referred to as Nightshade. In the past, there had been rumors of a creature sharing the Nightshade name as well. These plants are of a universal type, Eggplants and Tomatoes are part of the same family of nightshade, as well as Tabacco and the Belladonna plant. However, when herbalists refer to Nightshade they mainly refer to a specific type called Sunberries. It is used in home remedies by the common folk.

Sunset Walk.name: Sunset Walk
Sunset Walk.description: Sunset Walk is a generic wine found in most general stores. It got the name, more of an advertising technique to induce the idea that prior to going for the evening walk, have a glass of wine, as it boosts blood flow, for a more healthier life. Most herbalists and alchemists vehemently disagree. It’s not fine wine, but it warms the chest and softens the day’s sharp edges.

Tabasheer.name: Tabasheer
Tabasheer.description: Tabasheer is an opal-like type of stone found in the nodes of specific bamboo. This stone is not used for ornamentation but is valued by herbalists for medicinal purposes, aiding in the treatment of symptoms such as fevers, wounds, or uncontrollable vomiting, whether caused by arcane sources or otherwise.

Table.name: Table
Table.description: Standard tables are used to furnish both indoor and outdoor spaces of inns and taverns. They are usually made from planks for easy replacement and maintenance, providing surfaces for patrons to enjoy drinks and food.

Taffeta.name: Taffeta
Taffeta.description: A plain woven fabric made from silk, dressmakers, and seamstress mainly use it in ball gowns, wedding dresses, and corsets but it is also used in interior decoration as curtains and wall covers.

Teak Log.name: Teak Log
Teak Log.description: The logs are typically harvested from tropical regions and are known for their durability and resistance to rot and decay. The logs are often used to create strong and sturdy structures, such as buildings, bridges, and fences, as well as furniture. The wood is also prized for its resistance to pests and insects, making it a popular choice for use in household items.

Teak Plank.name: Teak Plank
Teak Plank.description: Because Teak wood is resistant to rot and decay, as well as insects and other pests, it is a popular choice for outdoor applications, such as decks, patios, and fencing. Teak can also be used in the construction of ships and other watercraft, as it is resistant to water damage and warping.

Tektite.name: Tektite
Tektite.description: When rocks fall from the heavens and smash mountains, the small-sized rocks that are charred are called tektites. However, most alchemists are curious about the overall process at the moment of impact, as it turns the small rocks into glass. Tektite is mainly used as furniture ornamentation as its shade of blue is quite pleasant to the eye.

The Flirt.name: The Flirt
The Flirt.description: Russula Vesca, or The Flirt, is an edible mushroom, in fact, many people consider this to be one of the very finest wild mushrooms. The mushroom itself does come with a mild nutty flavor and could be eaten even raw, however, they do shine when they are served with meat dishes or stews.

The Forest.name: The Forest
The Forest.description: The Forest is a necklace crafted from copper and set with a zoisite implement. While copper is a relatively inexpensive material, the presence of the zoisite balances the value, resulting in a piece that has a surprisingly high price. Yet, because copper is somewhat fragile, these necklaces are often found in crowded places, having slipped from their wearers' necks after the copper has corroded.

Thyme.name: Thyme
Thyme.description: Thyme is available in both fresh and dried forms. Although it's typically a summer-season herb, fresh greenhouse thyme can be found year-round. The fresh variety offers superior flavor but requires more careful handling, with a storage life usually not exceeding a week. However, it can be preserved for many months when frozen properly.

Tiger's Eye.name: Tiger's Eye
Tiger's Eye.description: One of the more common gemstones out there, used as a status symbol among commoners, as it comes in a combination of gold and black. Due to its high demand, depending on the region, it can be quite rare. In most superstitious groups the gemstone is assumed to repel undead and restless spirits.

Timed Explosive.name: Timed Explosive
Timed Explosive.description: Timed explosives are devices that detonate after a predetermined delay, often using a timer to trigger the explosion, giving just enough time to get out of harm's way. The delayed explosions allow fragmented rock to move before the next charge detonates, maximizing the blast's effectiveness and reducing vibrations that could damage the structure of the mines.

Tin.name: Tin
Tin.description: Tin is usually combined with Copper, due to it being soft, to prevent its oxidation. Doesn't have many uses besides the preservation of metals.

To-do list.name: To-do list
To-do list.description:
Basic list of things made by a commoner.

  1. Fetch water frum tha well ‘fore sun gets too high

  2. Check on the goats, make sure that gray one ain’t wandered off again

  3. Fix that busted fence post near tha turnip patch

  4. Go to market, see if Ol’ Markla still got flour what don’t got bugs in it

  5. Patch Pa’s boots, if I can find where the I put the needle

  6. Chop more firewood, cause it’s still cold an I don’t trust that sky none

  7. Say prayers so the crops don’t rot this year like last

  8. Scrub them rags what got blood on ‘em from the hog birthing

  9. Don’t forget to ask Jorren if he seen them bandits near the crossroads

  10. Feed the chickens, then make sure none got took by foxes again

Tomato.name: Tomato
Tomato.description: They come in multiple colors ranging from green to white, to yellow to red. Green tomatoes are usually stored over the winter with pickled cucumbers, as they have a very specific taste. The Red sweet, tangy tomatoes are delicious in all sorts of savory dishes, making it a must-have for most commoners and tavern keeps.

Tombak.name: Tombak
Tombak.description: Tombak is an alloy of copper and zinc used to imitate gold in cheap jewelry and for gilding. It is a cheap malleable alloy mainly used for medals, ornament, decoration and other ornamental uses. Yet the most nefarious of folk tend to purchase it in bulk to sell it at a premium as gold.

Topaz.name: Topaz
Topaz.description: Comes in many colors, one of the most common ones, is light blue, however, the most prized one is a bright yellow also known as Imperial Topaz. Highly valued by nobility as they usually are offered as gifts at state gatherings. The gems are cut only by the most skilled master gem cutters.

Training Dummy.description: While quite rare and often quickly replaced by living partners, training dummies serve the purpose of training new combatants in body positions and strike techniques. Due to their fragility and tendency to break with minimal use, they are rare.
Training Dummy.name: Training Dummy

Transport Cage.name: Transport Cage
Transport Cage.description: This wooden cage is designed for the easy transportation of small livestock, such as hens, chickens, and small hares, facilitating their movement to and from local markets with ease.

Travel Horse.name: Travel Horse
Travel Horse.description: A riding horse, or a saddle horse, is a horse used by mounted riders for recreation or transportation. It offers freedom, and movement, and enables some amazing feats of athleticism. There is a total thrill in galloping across an open field, in tune with your mount.

Tulip.name: Tulip
Tulip.description: Tulips once triggered a major economic crisis, known as "tulip mania," because of their striking appearance, popularity, and limited availability. Since they required a specific climate and harvest cycle, they became luxury goods. After the bubble burst, tulips eventually became accessible at a more reasonable price, accessible to every commoner.

Tuna Fish.name: Tuna Fish
Tuna Fish.description: The tuna is a saltwater fish, commonly found in warmer waters across the realms. It is widely harvested from the seas and oceans, with catches often reaching hundreds of pounds or more per fish, making it a valuable resource in many regions.

Turqoise.name: Turqoise
Turqoise.description: Carved or used as ornaments, turquoise has been considered a precious gem throughout history. Found in old tombs in the form of small animal statuettes or as an ornaments on doors for royalty. It is a common good luck charm, used by horsemen and traders while traveling to ward off any ambushes or missteps in their future travels.

Variscite.name: Variscite
Variscite.description: Variscite, resembling Jade in color, is commonly used as a decorative element in clothing. According to belief, it possesses toxic properties harmful to nocturnal creatures if introduced through wounds or ingestion.

Vegetable Box.name: Vegetable Box
Vegetable Box.description: The box is designed for storing and transporting vegetables and fruits. Its stackable design makes it quite convenient for easy transport.

Velvet.name: Velvet
Velvet.description: One of the most well known materials. It is woven on special types of looms that have a distinct way of seaming the cloth together. Velvet is often associated with nobility, however, in more recent times it got replaced with Cashmere wool.

Vermilion Snapper.name: Vermilion Snapper
Vermilion Snapper.description: Vermilion Snapper is a relatively large fish, typically caught by bottom fishing with live bait, such as squid or shrimp. The best time to catch Vermilion Snapper is between early summer and late fall when they are found over hard bottoms and reefs.

Vertebrae.name: Vertebrae
Vertebrae.description: Vertebrae are the multiple individual bones that interlock with each other to form the spinal column of most creatures. The most crafty craftsmen can create either flexible tools, like sturdy hook lines that can hold multiple creatures, or advanced weaponry, like bone whips that can do heavy damage to everything they touch.

Vertugal.name: Vertugal
Vertugal.description:
"Some say that the forest spirits don't like it when you cut these trees. As time has passed, more and more sightings of these beings have been made, taking the shape of different woodland creatures, from cats to foxes, deer to bears. If you notice one and you plan to bring harm to the forest, you won't be in this realm for long."

A type of soft, light-colored wood with a straight grain is commonly used in pulpwood and paper production. This wood is popular in the printing industry, as it is lightweight and easy to work with. It can also be used in combination with fabric, such as cotton, or animal leather, to create parchment or other paper products.

Vial of the Forgotten.name: Vial of the Forgotten
Vial of the Forgotten.description: This mysterious concoction is said to forge a powerful connection between the living and the dead. When consumed, the drinker gains the ability to interact with loved ones who have passed on or to gain insights from the spirits of the past. However, once consumed, this potion never wears off, slowly draining the sanity of those who dare to connect with the afterlife. From the few records that exist on this potion, it is also stated that you can see your own ghost, even while alive, making it uncertain if the potion is lethal.

Violet Garnet.description: Or Rhodolite, is mainly used by whitesmiths, jewelers, gaffers, and sometimes even blacksmiths for ornamentation purposes. In some cultures and faiths, garnets were thought to be the solidified blood of the religion's specific divine avatar.
Violet Garnet.name: Violet Garnet

Wall Meat.name: Wall Meat
Wall Meat.description: Wall Meat, harvested from the Kurnarac, is a prized delicacy among the subterranean folk, due to the long and laborious process of planting and harvesting it. Despite its name, Wall Meat is not actual meat but a type of mushroom with a tough, hard exterior. Its preparation is simple but can be off-putting to those unaccustomed to underground cuisine, as it has a strong, mold-like odor from its lengthy time spent in the subterranean environment. Only those with a robust constitution are fit to cook it. Once prepared, however, it has a rich flavor, blending the best qualities of tender beef and sautéed mushrooms.

Walnut Log.name: Walnut Log
Walnut Log.description: Walnut wood is commonly used for furniture, flooring, cabinetry, and decorative items due to its rich color and workability. The leaves can be used for sunburns, itching as well, and dandruff, when processed by the herbalists in poultrices.

Walnut Lumber.name: Walnut Lumber
Walnut Lumber.description: Once harvested, walnut lumber is commonly used for furniture, flooring, cabinetry, and decorative items due to its rich color and workability. and due to it being a low price, it is in high demand by most carpenters and woodworkers.

Watchful Gaze.name: Watchful Gaze
Watchful Gaze.description: Watchful Gaze is a cocktail with sweetened gelatin shaped to resemble an eye. It is primarily cooled vodka, however, the sugar from the gelatin and the rendered fat from the gelatin, it does provide a specific, quite famous, taste. Despite its eerie aesthetic, the cocktail is simple to make and relatively cheap, due to its minimal ingredient list.

Water Opal.name: Water Opal
Water Opal.description: Water Opal, sometimes referred to as jelly opal, is a precious opal known for its transparent appearance and absence of body color. Unlike typical opals, water opals have a unique property of seemingly dissipating arcane spells.

Wheel of Cheese.description: While some may argue that what beats a slice of cheese, in most scenarios, the answer is indeed a wheel of cheese, and fortunately, most markets and taverns offer it for sale.
Wheel of Cheese.name: Wheel of Cheese

White Beans.name: White Beans
White Beans.description: White beans are known for their pale color and starchy texture. Multiple types exist in various shapes and sizes, often bearing different names depending on the region. Though commonly used interchangeably in cooking, each variety offers a distinct size, form, and taste.

White Gold.name: White Gold
White Gold.description: White gold, an alloy of gold and silver, should not be mistaken for platinum. Known as gray gold in some regions, it is highly valued by the nobility for its high price and pristine silver look in their jewelry.

White Grub.name: White Grub
White Grub.description: White Grubs are the larvae form of multiple beetles, found just beneath the soil. They feed on the roots of grasses and plants, causing dead patches in pastures and gardens. Though a bane to most farms, anglers value them as exceptionally effective bait.

White Pearl.name: White Pearl
White Pearl.description: These specific Pearls are generally found in saltwater and are mostly sought after by royalty and nobility due to their rarity. The rarest ones, usually the size of someone's head are used in crystal balls, however, they mustn't have any imperfections.

White Radish.name: White Radish
White Radish.description: Quite often it is referred to as the winter radish due to its clear white color very similar to snow. The white radish works well in salads and slaws, or as a side dish for summer picnics, and also works well stir-fried with meat.

Whole Poultry.name: Whole Poultry
Whole Poultry.description: Many taverns offer a variety of stuffed poultry dishes, ideal for both special events and those seeking to share a hearty platter. These dishes are typically accompanied by sides such as roasted potatoes or greens, catering to diverse tastes.

Wine.name: Wine
Wine.description: While grape wine remains the most common variety, there are also other variants available, including blueberry, nettle, apple, elderberry, and red currant wines. Prices for these wines can vary significantly based on the fruit used and its age.

Witches Brew.name: Witches Brew
Witches Brew.description: Younglings adore it, and even some of the older folk take a mug for nostalgia, sometimes with a drop of spiced brandy slipped in by a cheeky tavern keeper. There are stories tied to it. Some say if you stir your cup thrice under the harvest moon you’ll glimpse the face of your future love in the foam. I tried once in Harthen Hollow. All I got was a sticky nose and a laugh from the brewer’s daughter who had been watching me the whole time. Maybe that counts as magic enough.

Witherite.name: Witherite
Witherite.description: Witherite is a distinctive gemstone known for its requirement of a specific mount. Whether used in jewelry, armor, or any decorative item, it must be mounted on an electrum piece, a gold and silver alloy, otherwise, it risks becoming dislodged.

Wolf Bane's Leaves.name: Wolf Bane's Leaves
Wolf Bane's Leaves.description: Referred to as Belladonna, it is usually believed to be of aid when communing with deities, as it is known that it can bring forth a strong connection to any plane. Alchemists usually harvest it with utmost caution as it is used in the creation of one of the deadliest plant-derived poisons on the market. Herbalists use it as a cure for early forms of Lycanthropy and as a treatment to manage and delay the more advanced stages of the disease.

Wolf Jaw Tongs.name: Wolf Jaw Tongs
Wolf Jaw Tongs.description: Considered the most universal tongs for general work of varying sizes, wolf jaw tongs feature smooth, narrow jaws on top with a V-notch on the inside running its length. This makes them one of the most essential tools in the tool belt of any blacksmith.

Wolfsbane Retreat.name: Wolfsbane Retreat
Wolfsbane Retreat.description: This potion is known to keep the Lycanthrope transformation at bay. The Wolf Bane leaves need to be fresh and be ground to a cloud of fine dust, then combined with a couple of Belladonna fruits and some other reagents. Anyone who is not a Lycanthrope will meet his death at the first drop of it on his tongue. If consumed by a Lycanthrope that was just bitten, it is speculated it can fully cure it. When it is consumed at the start of the evening of the full moon it can delay the transformation to the next full moon.

Wood Lily.name: Wood Lily
Wood Lily.description: Gathered from mountainous regions, they are said to be quite toxic for small home animals, however, herbalists use their bulbs crushed on swellings and bruises, or provide them as medicinal teas for patients who complain of stomachaches or coughs and fevers.

Wooden Mannequin.name: Wooden Mannequin
Wooden Mannequin.description: Wooden mannequins, also known as laymen or lay figures, have many uses, ranging from artists to leatherworkers. Artists utilize these mannequins as molds for their sculptures, applying a layer of gypsum that is later removed to reveal the final form. Meanwhile, leatherworkers shape leather around these figures to craft hide armor and other equipment tailored to fit a humanoid form.

Wooden Pipes.name: Wooden Pipes
Wooden Pipes.description: Wooden pipes, or tobacco pipes, are specifically designed for smoking tobacco and herbs. They are typically constructed from strong, hardwoods like briar or corn cob, each offering benefits such as durability and a unique smoking flavor.

Wooden Wheel.name: Wooden Wheel
Wooden Wheel.description: The wheel is a vital invention with widespread applications in society. It is used as a main component in carts, wagons, and carriages, and it is also been used in executions, such as with the breaking wheel. Its importance is evident in various aspects of a commoner's life.

Wool.name: Wool
Wool.description: A textile fabric that is obtained from farm animals, mainly sheep, and is primarily used for clothing. In addition to that, it is also used for blankets, saddle cloths for cavalry, carpeting, and on rare occasions even a soil fertilizer.

Wulfenite.name: Wulfenite
Wulfenite.description: Wulfenite is exceptionally soft and prone to breaking due to its flat, rectangular crystals. Despite this, its vibrant red and orange hues are captivating, leading gem cutters to produce small, sparkling, faceted gems ideal for settings with minimal handling, like inlays on furniture and royal displays.

Yam.name: Yam
Yam.description: The process of harvesting yams is physically demanding and labor-intensive, necessitating the use of wooden tools to protect the crop. Some farmers adopt staking and mixed cropping methods, which can complicate the harvesting process. Yams are utilized in diverse culinary preparations, such as flour or whole vegetable pieces for salads or stews.

Yellowfin Tuna.name: Yellowfin Tuna
Yellowfin Tuna.description: Yellowfin tuna is a large and powerful oceanic fish distinguished by its glowing yellow fins and sleek, torpedo-shaped body built for speed and endurance, making it a most difficult catch. Found mostly in warm tropical waters near secluded islands, it demands the use of specially equipped fishing boats with trolleys to handle its immense weight.

Zamac.name: Zamac
Zamac.description: Zamac is a zinc-based alloy primarily used in jewelry, but it is also utilized for making tools, bolts, and hinges. It is particularly favored in applications where paint is used, as dyes adhere exceptionally well to it.

Zircon.name: Zircon
Zircon.description: Zircon is a gemstone of many colors. There is also a transparent variant of it very similar to a diamond, which is why its usually used as a cheaper alternative to it. Anyone without knowledge of gemstones can be easily fooled into believing that the stone is more expensive than its actually is.

Abjurating Abjuratives in Abjuration.name: 防护学派的驱散器具 Abjurating Abjuratives in Abjuration
Abjurating Abjuratives in Abjuration.description: “防护（Abjuration）是指通过宣誓所进行的郑重否认、放弃或弃绝，通常指对公民身份或某项权利与特权的放弃。”这段话以醒目的粗体字写在第一页上。接下来的内容列举了所使用的材料示例，以及由此产生的各类法术变化。本书似乎由一位吟游诗人所著，旨在加深读者对防护学派的理解，以及其在奥术知识中的具体应用。

Advanced Military Application of the Arc.name: 奥术艺术的高级军事应用 Advanced Military Application of the Arcane Arts
Advanced Military Application of the Arc.description: 这是一本小册子，深入探讨了奥术技艺在战术层面的军事运用。书中包含多个章节，涵盖了诸如小队编组、侧翼包抄技巧、在敌后迷失时的生存建议、基础生存技能，以及在最后一章中强调的指挥链重要性等战斗主题。本手册会发放给新招募的士兵，并要求他们记住其中全部200页的内容。

Agave.name: 龙舌兰 Agave
Agave.description: 龙舌兰植物的四个主要可食用部分包括花、叶、茎（或基生莲座）以及汁液。其烘烤后的纤维可以压榨出饮品，而其根茎部分则可以通过烘烤或煮制后捣成薄片，晒干并储存以备后用。经过烘干处理的头部也可以再次煮制并加工成可食用的糊状物，可直接食用，或加入汤品之中。

Agni Manitite.name: 阿格尼玛尼石 Agni Manitite
Agni Manitite.description: 阿格尼玛尼石在市场上也被称为“火之珍珠”，是一种仅在少数岛屿上发现的极为稀有的玻陨石。这些石头源自坠入大型水体的陨石，在迅速冷却过程中形成。由于这种独特的生成过程，使得它们极难被回收获取。

Alabaster.name: 雪花石膏 Alabaster
Alabaster.description: 雪花石膏质地柔软，易于雕刻成复杂精美的造型，因此成为雕塑创作中广受欢迎的材料。它也常被用于装饰性建筑工程，例如制作雕像、花瓶以及各种小型装饰雕刻品。

Alexandrite.name: 亚历山大变石 Alexandrite
Alexandrite.description: 人们相信，如果这枚透明宝石的持有者行善积德或守护家人，它便会赐予其好运。在某些地区，这种宝石被视为神圣之物，常被用作献祭给神祇，或用于为场所进行祝圣。

Almond.name: 杏仁 Almond
Almond.description: 杏仁通常以生食或烘烤的形式出售，也被用于制作杏仁奶、杏仁油、杏仁酱、杏仁粉、杏仁膏以及杏仁糖等产品。

Aluminum Ore.name: 铝矿 Aluminum Ore
Aluminum Ore.description: 铝矿是一种轻质金属，可用作合金材料。由于其质地柔软，铁匠会将其与银矿结合来打造餐具或容器，而炼金术师则会将其用于制作某些独特药剂。

Amaranth.name: 苋 Amaranth
Amaranth.description: 苋，通常被称为红根苋，主要用于收获谷物，并进一步加工成面粉。由于其快速的爆发式生长，常采用“三姐妹农业法”与其一同种植，从而在收获时获得多种作物的混合产出。

Amber.name: 琥珀 Amber
Amber.description: 琥珀是松树树脂的化石，可经过抛光后用于装饰用途，如珠宝饰品的珠子。炼金术师对琥珀有很高的需求，因为它能够封存一些被遗忘的小型生物。

Amblygonite.name: 磷铝石 Amblygonite
Amblygonite.description: 透明的磷铝石最为稀有，可被切割成宝石，但由于其硬度和韧性较低，在作为首饰佩戴时容易破裂和磨损。根据民间传说，让这种宝石在舌头上溶解被认为能够治疗内伤。

Aradite.name: 阿拉石 Aradite
Aradite.description: 阿拉石宝石具有在黑暗中发光的独特能力，使其成为许多地下文明常用的光源。与霓隆菇一同，由于其储量丰富而被频繁使用。

Aragonite.name: 霰石 Aragonite
Aragonite.description: 霰石是最难切割的宝石之一，需要技艺高超的宝石切割师才能将其加工成装饰性宝石。有传闻称这些石头在近距离时会干扰魔法物品，但此类说法通常被认为缺乏依据。

Aurichalcum Ingot.name: 奥里哈刚锭 Aurichalcum Ingot
Aurichalcum Ingot.description: 当杂质被去除并制成纯净的锭块后，奥里哈刚锭被广泛用于齿轮、船只部件、叶轮、轴承、金属套筒及其他机械零件等关键组件中。奥里哈刚仍然延续着其古老传统，人们依旧会铸造这种金属的货币。

Aurichalcum Ore.name: 奥里哈刚矿 Aurichalcum Ore
Aurichalcum Ore.description: 在大地深处的矿脉中蕴藏着奥里哈刚，这是一种由锌与铜构成的合金，闪耀如黄铜，却拥有堪比黄金的价值。在极为罕见的条件下，当压力积聚、时间延展，这种合金会结晶为奥里哈刚石。由奥里哈刚铸造的货币曾被视为珍贵的财富，在久远的文明中象征着繁荣与权力。

Aventurine.name: 东陵石 Aventurine
Aventurine.description: 虽然绿色是东陵石最常见的颜色，但它也可以呈现橙色、棕色、黄色、蓝色或灰色等多种色调。东陵石被广泛用于景观与建筑石材、水族装饰、纪念碑以及珠宝制作等领域。

Avocado.name: 牛油果 Avocado
Avocado.description: 牛油果具有多种用途，从治疗疾病到烹饪皆有涉及。有时它会被切成薄片或制成糊状，据说能够让皮肤焕发光泽。草药师对其美容用途尚存疑，但普遍认为食用是相当安全的。

Axinite.name: 斧石 Axinite
Axinite.description: 斧石宝石的不幸之处在于其呈棕色，这种颜色在宝石中并不受欢迎。尽管如此，它们常被用于制作戒指，因为能够保持锋利的边缘，并能承受酒馆斗殴中的冲击与磨损。这也使它们获得了“指节石”的绰号。

Balista.description: 弩炮是一种主要用于堡垒和定居点防御的武器。它的运作原理不同于普通弩，使用带有扭力弹簧的双臂结构，而非依赖弓臂张力。有传闻称存在一种更为先进的武器，称为连发弩炮，不过这些说法尚未得到证实。
Balista.name: 弩炮 Ballista

Barrel.name: 木桶 Barrel
Barrel.description: 木桶是最常见的储存容器之一，尤其适用于盛放液体。其用途从发酵烈酒到腌制食物或储存自制果汁不等，因此成为大容量储存的首选容器。

Basalt Stone.name: 玄武岩 Basalt Stone
Basalt Stone.description: 玄武岩主要呈深灰色，常用于建筑中的砌块或地基材料，也用于铺设鹅卵石道路，以及由著名石匠雕刻成雕像。

Bay Leaf.name: 月桂叶 Bay Leaf
Bay Leaf.description: 月桂叶是一种芳香叶片，常作为烹饪用香草使用。它能为菜肴增添细腻的草本风味，可整片使用，无论是干燥还是新鲜皆可。通常在食用前会将其从菜中取出。月桂叶也可以磨成粉末使用，但较为少见。

Basil.name: 罗勒 Basil
Basil.description: 罗勒是一种在诸界各地料理中常见的香料。据说食用后能够安抚并治愈心脏，但尚无草药师证实其具有任何疗效。它常被平民广泛使用。

Bean Stew with Meat.name: 肉豆炖菜 Bean Stew with Meat
Bean Stew with Meat.description: 大量熏制肉类会提前准备，因为它们是大多数豆类炖菜的重要原料。在火堆上的铸铁锅中，将所有食材（包括熏肉）一同加入，以小火慢慢炖煮，通常还会配上一份辛辣的辣椒作为佐餐。

Bearberry.name: 熊果 Bearberry
Bearberry.description: 许多采集者、草药师和炼金术师会在工作场所附近种植熊果，据说它们能够吸引蜜蜂和蜂鸟，从而有助于授粉与产蜜，并吸引蝴蝶以便采集其翅膀。熊果果实在医学中的应用较为有限，主要用于药用茶。此外，熊果也被制革匠用作鞣制剂，将兽皮转化为皮革。

Bedside Chest.name: 床头箱 Bedside Chest
Bedside Chest.description: 对于负担不起床头柜或衣柜的平民来说，床头箱提供了一种经济实惠的替代方案，可用于存放各类衣物。

Beer.name: 啤酒 Beer
Beer.description: 大多数酒馆、酒吧和修道院都会自行酿造啤酒以满足当地需求。由于啤酒可以低成本大规模生产，它始终为这些场所提供稳定的收益。

Beet.name: 甜菜 Beet
Beet.description: 甜菜有时也被称为甜菜根。据传红色甜菜根会被用于增强饮用者活力的药剂，人们认为这是因为其红色外观所致，但这些说法并无根据。草药师与炼金术师确实会在药剂中使用甜菜，但并非为了其颜色，而是因为其所带来的健康益处。

Benitoite.name: 蓝锥矿 Benitoite
Benitoite.description: 蓝锥矿晶体以相对较软著称，通常以碎片形式被发现，而非完整晶体。它们常被用作镶嵌材料，用于宝箱、装饰盒以及小型雕像之中。

Birch Log.name: 桦木原木 Birch Log
Birch Log.description: 桦木原木是在诸界中常见且重要的材料，以其多用途与实用性而备受珍视。它是一种从广泛生长的桦树上采集而来的细长浅色原木。其木质坚韧且富有韧性，使其适用于多种用途。工匠与手艺人都十分看重桦木原木，因为它易于加工塑形，可被制作成各种物品。从简单的工具和家居用品，到复杂的雕刻与精致的家具，都常以其为材料。

Bismuth Bronze.name: 铋青铜 Bismuth Bronze
Bismuth Bronze.description: 铋青铜（或称铋黄铜）是一种铜合金，主要用于制造耐腐蚀的螺栓、钉子以及各种金属部件。其独特特性使其成为海洋环境中的理想材料。

Black Pearl.name: 黑珍珠 Black Pearl
Black Pearl.description: “黑珍珠”这一称呼仅是描述性的，因为人们已知可以通过染色来使珍珠呈现这种特定的深色。在某些宗教中，人们相信发现这种珍珠象征着神祇的眷顾或不悦，具体取决于当时的情境。

Black Sapphire.name: 黑蓝宝石 Black Sapphire
Black Sapphire.description: 黑蓝宝石在经过切割后，常被称为“星彩蓝宝石”，其反射会形成明显的六射星形光芒。其魔法特性如今几乎已被遗忘，但人们普遍认为它与时间的流逝以及对时间的操控有关。

Blewits.name: 紫蜡蘑 Blewits
Blewits.description: 紫蜡蘑是一种风味浓郁且美味的蘑菇，可通过其淡紫色外观轻松辨认。它们常在秋季出现在覆盖物堆中，适合炒制食用。它们经常被误认为另一种名为“紫水晶欺诈菇”的蘑菇，不过这两种都可以食用。

Boiled Corn.name: 水煮嫩玉米 Boiled Corn
Boiled Corn.description: 在玉米完全成熟之前采摘的嫩玉米，可以加盐后烧烤或水煮。这种时令美味只在收获季之前短暂供应，因其独特且应季的风味而需求极高。

Bolt.name: 螺栓 Bolt
Bolt.description: 螺栓、螺母和螺钉在生活的各个方面都不可或缺，它们被用于制作工具、锁具、时钟、家具，甚至用于固定坚固的金属设备部件。贵族，尤其是身着重型护甲的人，常会雇佣一名随行的护甲匠。该技艺娴熟的匠人会随身携带必要工具，如锤子、备用螺栓、钢丝剪和拔钉器，以确保无论行至何处，贵族的护甲始终保持最佳状态。

Book of Pawns.name: 双关笑话集 Book of Pawns
Book of Pawns.description: 《双关笑话集》（“Book of Pawns”）是一部名称诙谐的书籍，收录了大量双关语笑话，足以让再严肃的人也忍不住发出无奈的叹息。典型例子包括：“如果你向树人寻求人生建议会发生什么？它会告诉你要‘向外分枝，然后离开（branch out and leave）’。”以及“为什么狗头人的单口表演失败了？——因为他们的笑话太‘拖（drag on）’了。”

Box of Horrors.name: 惊怖糖盒 Box of Horrors
Box of Horrors.description: 它们只会在“赤收节”期间出现，那时夜晚渐长，空气中弥漫着烟火与苹果酒的气息。孩子们整年都在期待这些盒子，因为里面装满了甜食，他们会在篝火之间穿梭，手指黏腻、嘴唇染成紫色。成年商人假装对此不屑一顾，但我见过不止一位商队首领在营地安顿好之前，就“检查”完整整一盒。

Braided Bread.name: 编结面包 Braided Bread
Braided Bread.description: 编结面包在许多地区都是一种传统食物，其象征意义因地域而异。在某些地方，它与对逝者的哀悼相关，人们会将一条编结面包放置在墓地，以象征在彼界的联系；在另一些地区，它象征新婚夫妇的结合，面包的编结代表着交织的双臂与爱情；而在其他地方，它则不具备任何特殊的文化意义。

Brass.name: 黄铜 Brass
Brass.description: 黄铜与青铜类似，后者是一种由铜和锡构成的合金。由于其明亮的金色外观，黄铜是一种非常受欢迎的装饰材料。它的用途广泛，从锁具、铰链和齿轮，到管道与阀门，以及号角或铃铛等乐器的制作。

Bread.name: Bread is a staple food made from dough that has been baked. It comes in a variety of shapes, sizes, and flavors. It is often sold as a side in most taverns and inns for any of the main meals they serve.
Bread.description: 面包是一种由面团烘焙而成的主食，具有多种形状、大小和风味。在大多数酒馆和旅店中，它常作为配餐与各类主菜一同出售。

Bread Buns.name: 小面包 Bread Buns
Bread Buns.description: 小面包也被称为餐包，通常会被切开并夹入各种食材制成各类三明治。多数地方会将其作为炖菜或汤品的配餐提供。

Breadseed Poppy.name: 罂粟 Breadseed Poppy
Breadseed Poppy.description: 罂粟是一种常见于花园中的观赏植物，同时也因其种子、油脂以及药用价值而被栽培。与烈酒混合后，可用作强效止痛剂。

Broadcloth.description: 细平布‌是最常见的一种布料，通常也被称为普通布。传统上广泛用于各类服装制作，同时也用于室内装饰，如桌布、窗帘以及墙面覆盖物。
Broadcloth.name: 细平布‌ Broadcloth

Brocade.name: 织锦 Brocade
Brocade.description: 贵族的生活充满了娱乐、骑马、狩猎、音乐与舞蹈，而这些活动也成为展示时尚的舞台。富有的贵族常穿着织锦服饰，这些衣物由多色丝绸制成，有时还会嵌入金银丝线，以彰显其财富。

Broccoli.name: 西兰花 Broccoli
Broccoli.description: 西兰花属于甘蓝类植物，通常呈绿色、紫色或白色。最常见的做法是水煮，不过蒸制、烤制或翻炒同样美味。

Brooch.name: 胸针 Brooch
Brooch.description: 胸针是一种用于固定衣物的装饰性饰品。它通常由金属制成，如银或金，也可能由其他材料制成，如黏土。胸针常以银丝或宝石装饰，既可作为纯粹的装饰品，也能发挥固定衣物的实用功能。

Brotherhood of the North.name: 北境兄弟会 Brotherhood of the North
Brotherhood of the North.description: 这本书被认定为一部历史文献，其大部分内容尚未被完全翻译。历史学家在每次修订时都会尝试根据最新发现更新其译文，但它似乎是以一种数千年前便已灭绝的语言写成。根据目前可得的译文，书中内容主要由描写北方领土战争的诗歌构成。然而，即便是这些解读也存在激烈争议，因为翻译的准确性仍然无法确定。

Bucket.name: 水桶 Bucket
Bucket.description: 水桶通常用于将水从井中运送到家中或用于清洁工作，但在极端情况下，或在某些没有厕所设施的牢房中，也会被临时用作便桶。

Burger.name: 汉堡 Burger
Burger.description: 汉堡是将一块烤制的牛肉饼夹在对半切开的面包之间制成的。还可以加入生洋葱片、生菜、培根以及蛋黄酱等配料，以增强风味。

Cabbage.name: 卷心菜 Cabbage
Cabbage.description: 卷心菜是许多沙拉和炖菜的主要原料，旅店和酒馆通常对其需求很高，用于为顾客准备晚餐。大多数家庭农场都会种植几棵卷心菜，而且人们常将其用盐腌制成酸菜，以便在冬季食用。

Cacoxenite.name: 柯可克森石 Cacoxenite
Cacoxenite.description: 柯可克森石通常更多与铁矿石相关，而非宝石；然而，由于其不自然的金色、橙色或深红色光泽，它同样可以被珠宝匠用于制作精巧而色彩丰富的饰品，也可被铁匠用于打造精美华丽的武器。

Calendar.name: 日历 Calendar
Calendar.description: 一种用于记录多个季节天数的简单工具，通过符号或图画进行清晰标示，使普通人也能轻松了解当前季节中的具体日期，以及距离季节结束还有多长时间。它可以用于记录诸如收获、春季或其他节庆等特定事件。

Capricia Verde.name: 卡普里西亚维德 Capricia Verde
Capricia Verde.description: 卡普里西亚维德是一种以朗姆酒为基底的烈性饮品，通常还会加入橙子、橙叶和肉桂等配料。无论具体配方如何，它都象征着情谊与团结。水手们常在庆祝、告别或劫后余生之时传递装满这种饮品的酒杯，将其视作共享团结与友谊的仪式。

Carambola.name: 杨桃 Carambola
Carambola.description: 杨桃果肉饱满、口感清脆多汁，带有轻微的酸涩与甜味。其外形呈星形，颜色为金黄色，常用于制作水果沙拉和果盘，也常作为鸡尾酒和饮品的装饰，或榨成果汁作为功能性饮料饮用。

Carbon Steel.name: 碳钢 Carbon Steel
Carbon Steel.description: 由于颜色相近却缺乏其奥术特性，碳钢常被误认为是暗钢。这种合金是钢的一种非常坚固的变体，但也更容易生锈。它主要用于制造切削工具、弹簧、扳手、锤子以及其他各类建筑工具。

Carbuncle.name: 红榴石 Carbuncle
Carbuncle.description: 红榴石，也被称为红色石榴石，是用于各类珠宝中最为经济实惠的宝石之一。据说，如果将其镶嵌并附魔于武器之上，该武器的攻击将能够击杀任何敌人。

Carpenters Plane.name: 木工刨 Carpenters Plane
Carpenters Plane.description: 刨子是一种配有巧妙固定刀刃的工具，专门用于刨削或修整木材表面。它主要用于表面精加工与找平，能够有效去除斧头、锤子或锯子等早期工具留下的痕迹，使木材变得光滑、平整且笔直。

Carriage Safety Guide.name: 马车安全指南 Carriage Safety Guide
Carriage Safety Guide.description:
一份小型安全卷轴，包含下一次马车旅程的相关信息，内容包括：

  • 吸烟区：我们理解这可能会引起误解，但该区域并非用于食用异域草药，而是专为元素性质的类人生物设立。请勿在该区域进行任何形式的熄灭行为。

  • 金属、沉重及大型生物：若你是由金属构成、体型巨大，或是携带大量紧凑储存物品（例如额外位面空间储物）的施法者，请在登车前告知我们。可能需要配备专门的驮兽。未提前通知将收取额外行李费用。

  • 具爪类人生物：请勿抓挠任何座椅或马车木制部分。

  • 具角类人生物：除非你的角向内弯曲，否则必须始终为角套上保护塞。

  • 变化系施法者：请勿将任何液体转化为酒精。由于先前发生过事故，本次旅程中严禁任何酒精。此外，请忽略旅途中遇到的任何会尖叫的岩石。

  • 满月提示：具有狼人症状的乘客不建议在满月期间预订行程。若你选择无视此警告，请注意你可能会被永久雇佣，以替换旅途中损失的驮兽。

  • 灵光持有者：具有活跃灵光的乘客必须停留在指定区域内。我们理解你所信仰的宗教、神祇或契约的重要性，但我们也需考虑所有旅客的舒适，包括来自其他文化的人，或刚从来世归来的人。不遵守者将被报告至最近的卫兵岗哨。

  • 石化能力生物：若你的种族能够通过目光或其他方式使他人石化，必须在登车前通知工作人员。未告知者将在旅程期间于座位周围安装镜子。

  • 进食规定：旅途中禁止进食。我们会提供多个休息站供你用餐。无论文化习俗如何，其他乘客均不被视为食物。

  • 安全提示：请始终将四肢置于马车内。我们对旅途中发生的任何伤害概不负责。

Steel.name: Steel
Steel.description:
"Well, the building block of life, or so they say. I do love its glimmer in the night, especially when guardsmen wear their polished armors, keeps me safe, you know?"

Created from slabs of iron ore in high heat furnaces with added charcoal, it creates one of the sturdiest metals for weaponry. Steel is used mainly in cutlery, inexpensive rings, armor, and weaponry.

Stargazer Lily.description:
"As dusk falls and the night sky begins to darken, there’s a sense of anticipation in the air. Those who know the legend watch the sky closely, waiting for the first signs of a meteor shower. At that very moment, the Stargazer Lily begins to bloom, its petals unfolding as if responding to the celestial display above. The sweet scent fills the air, carrying with it the magic of the stars as if the flower itself has been touched by the heavens."

The Stargazer Lily, known for its fragrance, is grown and harvested in various regions to create perfumes. Some believe that the flower blooms only during a meteor shower, adding a mystical element to its already enchanting nature.
Stargazer Lily.name: Stargazer Lily

Stibiotantalite.name: Stibiotantalite
Stibiotantalite.description: Stibiotantalite is primarily valued as a gemstone, known for its vivid, shiny, golden-brown appearance. It was commonly worn by merchants and mid-level courtiers as an affordable alternative to more expensive gemstones.

Stool.name: Stool
Stool.description: A standard wooden stool used in many taverns is often repurposed as an improvised weapon in the event of a brawl.

Strawberry Blakewell.name: Strawberry Blakewell
Strawberry Blakewell.description: The Strawberry Blakewell is a type of tart made with a shortcrust pastry shell layered with jam, frangipane, and a topping of flaked almonds and candied fruit. Although it is mostly associated with strawberries, other candied fruits such as cherries, kiwis, pears, and peaches can be used as well. This tart is a popular snack among people of all ages due to its sweet and fruity flavor.

Cashmere wool.name: 开司米羊绒 Cashmere Wool
Cashmere wool.description: 通常简称为“开司米”，是一种主要取自特定品种山羊的纤维。开司米可用于制作各种服饰，其中最常见的是长袍，这类服装逐渐被中产阶级平民作为身份地位的象征而使用。

Cassiterite.name: 锡石 Cassiterite
Cassiterite.description: 锡石在珠宝匠与铁匠之间一直存在争议：它究竟应被归类为宝石，还是类似锡的矿石。不过，双方一致认为它具有很高的延展性，且易于加工。

Catfish.name: 鲶鱼 Catfish
Catfish.description: 鲶鱼因其独特的须状触须而得名，是钓鱼者喜爱的目标鱼类。通常使用活饵最容易钓到鲶鱼，不过鲱鱼块或鸡肝同样有效。在厨师的手中，鲶鱼在各类海鲜菜肴中表现出色，无论是炸至金黄酥脆，还是与柠檬、莳萝和大蒜等香草香料一起煎制，都十分美味。

Cendal.name: 森达尔丝 Cendal
Cendal.description: 一种轻质丝织物，主要用于制作礼仪服装和教会祭服，也常用于装饰旗帜。

Chair.name: 椅子 Chair
Chair.description: 木制椅子常见于住宅、商铺、旅店或酒馆，通常由木板制成，以便于更换部件。这在繁忙的场所尤为实用，因为在诸如斗殴等事件之后，椅子往往需要被替换。

Chalcedony.name: 玉髓 Chalcedony
Chalcedony.description: 据推测，它有时会被用于死灵术中，主要作为人类骨骼的替代品，用于为不死生物提供保护或施加控制。它也常被用于雕刻小型浮雕或半身像，并嵌入可开启的吊坠之中，主要用于珠宝制作。

Chalk.name: 粉笔 Chalk
Chalk.description: 粉笔是一种土质石灰岩，通常呈浅色，因其多种用途而备受青睐。它可用于在石板上书写或在野外留下标记，也可作为粉末用于划定运动场地边界。裁缝和其他布料工匠也会用粉笔在皮革或织物上绘制线条和图案，以便更精确地裁剪和缝制材料。此外，粉笔还常用于占卜，或在地面及墙壁、门等表面绘制魔法符号与法阵，用于阻挡不受欢迎的入侵者或束缚被召唤的生物。

Strudel.name: Strudel
Strudel.description: The strudel is a classic pastry, oblong in shape, with a fruit filling inside, such as apples, pears, peaches, berries, and many more. It can be topped with powdered sugar, sweet cocoa, or honey. This pastry is a quick favorite among people of all ages and pairs well with morning beverages, such as coffee or teas.

Sugar Beet.name: Sugar Beet
Sugar Beet.description: These types of beets are mainly cultivated by farmers, and with the help of herbalists turned into sugar.

Chalk Limestone Ashlar.name: 粉笔石灰岩方石 Chalk Limestone Ashlar
Chalk Limestone Ashlar.description: 由技艺精湛的石匠打造，这些石块被制成用于建筑外立面装饰的板材，或在少数情况下作为地砖使用。其浅色外观使其成为教堂、大教堂或石制王室庄园装饰的热门选择。然而，由于其作为铺装材料的耐久性有限，在较近时期逐渐被大理石或花岗岩所取代。

Cheese.name: 奶酪 Cheese
Cheese.description: 奶酪是一种由牛奶凝结并经过熟成制成的乳制品。它具有多种风味和质地，常作为调味品或烹饪食材使用，例如用于馅饼、糕点以及其他烘焙食品中。

Cherry.name: 樱桃 Cherry
Cherry.description:
“当温暖的季节来临、樱桃树盛开之时，在某些文化中，人们会在这些树下锻炼身心。他们相信先祖之灵会指引并协助他们的修行。”

樱桃是各地年轻人喜爱的水果之一。它们可以制成果酱、馅饼，甚至自制果汁，但最常见的食用方式仍是直接生吃。

Chess Pieces.name: 棋子 Chess Pieces
Chess Pieces.description: 雕刻棋子是一项复杂的工作，需要精湛的工艺以保持整套棋子风格的一致性。不同类型的棋类（如信使棋或帖木儿棋）需要不同的棋子设计，这也为制作过程增添了更多的复杂性与技艺要求。

Chicken and Rice.name: 鸡肉米饭 Chicken and Rice
Chicken and Rice.description: 这是大多数旅店和酒馆中的主食之一，因为它制作简单、价格低廉，而且是一顿相当饱腹的餐食。

Chisel.name: 凿子 Chisel
Chisel.description: 凿子是一种切削工具，被多种从事重型材料加工的行业使用，用于修整、塑形或加工木材、石材或金属等坚固材料。通常会配合木槌敲击使用，以确保加工的精确性。

Chitin.name: 几丁质 Chitin
Chitin.description: 在地下村落与城市中，几丁质是一种十分珍贵的材料，因为它既坚固又轻便。它通常来源于被猎杀的类昆虫生物，这些生物游荡于黑暗之中。虽然也存在专门培育几丁质的养殖场，但任何由冒险者带回的额外材料都备受欢迎，因为熟练的工匠可以将其制成有价值的工具、武器、服装甚至护甲。

Sushi.name: Sushi
Sushi.description: Sushi is made out of vinegared rice, usually seasoned with sugar and salt, combined with various ingredients like vegetables and meats, primarily seafood. It is commonly wrapped in dried seaweed and can be served either raw or cooked.

Sweet Bread.name: Sweet Bread
Sweet Bread.description: Sweet bread varies significantly in taste and appearance depending on the region. Some regions opt for cocoa, while others prefer fruit-based recipes. However, regardless of the specific type ordered, whether in taverns or bakeries, sweet bread is a popular choice for religious celebrations or events, as well as for those with a sweet tooth.

Sweetsop.name: Sweetsop
Sweetsop.description: It is commonly referred to as Sugar-Apple and is considered one of the most delicious, if not somewhat rare, fruits you can find. With its plump green scales, the sugar apple looks like a curled up pangolin. The sugar apple is usually consumed as a fresh dessert fruit, or used to make juice.

Tablàh.name: Tablàh
Tablàh.description: The tablàh, commonly referred to as the goblet drum or chalice drum, and sometimes called dombek or tarabuka depending on the region, has a shape that resembles a large goblet. Made of wood with a thin leather membrane, this percussion instrument plays a vital role in many musical traditions, contributing a unique rhythm to them.

Tambourine.name: Tambourine
Tambourine.description: The tambourine is a versatile percussion instrument featuring a wooden frame with pairs of small metal jingles known as zills. It can be played in many ways such as held in the hand, mounted on a stand, shaken to produce a lively sound, or struck sharply with the hand or a stick. Musicians can also use the tambourine attached to their leg or hip for added rhythm.

Tanzanite.name: Tanzanite
Tanzanite.description: The Tanzanite gemstone, a type of zoisite, is known for its deep blue color and unique optical properties. It is one of the few gemstones that can display three different colors depending on the viewing angle. Tanzanite appears as a distinct blue when viewed from one direction and shifts to hues ranging from violet to red when viewed from other angles.

Tea.name: Tea
Tea.description: Tea comes in various types such as green, black, root, herbal, or fruity, each served with hot water to achieve different desired effects. Herbalists often recommend peppermint or chamomile tea for its calming effects, while ginger tea is favored for alleviating stomach ailments. Black tea serves as a popular substitute for coffee, while green or fruity teas are enjoyed as refreshing beverages. Regardless of the type or purpose, tea is widely consumed and in high demand.

Chromite Ingots.name: 铬铁锭 Chromite Ingots
Chromite Ingots.description: 当矿石被冶炼成铬铁锭后，通常用于制造适应极端环境（无论是自然还是更偏魔法性质）的坚固工具。炼金术士有时也会使用从该矿石中提炼出的纯化金属，作为催化剂来调制能够赋予元素抗性的药剂。

Chrysoberyl.description: 所有有色透明的金绿宝石都会被加工为宝石。然而，由于其切割困难且价值相对有限，它们很少被用于珠宝制作。它们主要在魔法技艺和草药学中作为材料使用。
Chrysoberyl.name: 金绿宝石 Chrysoberyl

Chrysoprase.name: 绿玉髓 Chrysoprase
Chrysoprase.description: 绿玉髓的颜色从海玻璃般的浅绿到深祖母绿色不等，一些样本还呈现柠檬绿般的黄绿色调。它常被切割成水滴形、泪滴形或椭圆形，成为家具装饰和珠宝制作中颇受欢迎的宝石。此外，将其研磨后还可用于制作隐形墨水。

Cinnamon.name: 肉桂 Cinnamon
