Cinnamon.description: 肉桂取自多种树木的内层树皮，是一种广泛使用的芳香调味品与香料。它能够提升甜味与咸味菜肴的风味，也常用于贝果、茶、热巧克力以及各类传统食品中。

Clay Brick.name: 黏土砖 Clay Brick
Clay Brick.description: 黏土通过压入木制模具中成型为砖块，然后在窑炉中烧制而成。它们还可以上釉，呈现多彩的表面，或装饰以精细的图案，使其成为常见的装饰性建筑材料。

Clinozoisite.name: 绿帘石 Clinozoisite
Clinozoisite.description: 绿帘石，又被称为蜜酒石，据说能够保护佩戴者免受酒精中毒的影响。然而，由于人们对其是否真的具备这种能力存在不同看法，它也被称为断片石。

Cobnut.name: 榛子 Cobnut
Cobnut.description: 榛子可用于多种菜肴中，从馅饼、曲奇等烘焙食品，到制成酱汁用于点缀各类菜肴，再到各种形式的沙拉中都有应用。

Cocoa Beans.name: 可可豆 Cocoa Beans
Cocoa Beans.description: 可可豆生长于可可果荚之中，可以被研磨成细粉或切碎成小块，用于多种烹饪用途，从沙拉酱料到蛋糕配料，再到制作巧克力。可可豆经过干燥处理，具有较长的保存期限，因此也是各地贸易中的重要商品。

Coconut.name: 椰子 Coconut
Coconut.description: 椰子是椰子树结出的可食用果实。其果肉（也称椰肉）因可提取椰子油而备受重视。椰肉有时也会被直接食用，并作为旅行者日常口粮的一部分与其他食物搭配。

Codex of Hatred & Love.name: 憎恨与爱之书 Codex of Hatred & Love
Codex of Hatred & Love.description: 这本由瓦伦丁·沃尔夫维署名的小型手札，记录了他对于“爱”与“憎恨”在艺术与研究等不同领域中各自作用的思考，同时他完全否定了“悔恨”的价值。书中若干摘录表明，在所爱之人的目光中，诞生了历史上最美丽的肖像；而在仇恨的驱使下，人们则进行最深入的研究，只为驳倒对立的观点。至于悔恨，他仅留下这样一句话：“唯有已成之事，无法更改。”

The Last Veiled Widow.name: The Last Veiled Widow
The Last Veiled Widow.description: This highly acidic mushroom gets its name from the phosphorescent mesh that surrounds it. Compared to its more common counterpart, the Veiled Lady, the lethality of this specific mushroom is uncommon, as when ingested it turns to acid and starts consuming the unfortunate folk from the inside out, only leaving bones behind. A few months later, the bones give rise to more Veiled Widows.

The Sickener.name: The Sickener
The Sickener.description: The Sickener, or Vomiting Russula, is a mushroom that walks the line between danger and culinary delight. Its consumption can lead to nausea and vomiting, hence its grim nickname. Yet, some regions use it as a spice in traditional dishes like goulash, relying on careful preparation to neutralize its unpleasant properties.

Thimble.name: Thimble
Thimble.description: Resembling a small cap, typically made of metal or bone, the thimble is worn over the fingertip to safeguard it while pushing a needle through cloth during sewing. A cherished tool among tailors and leatherworkers, as it is indispensable in their toolset.

Coffee.name: 咖啡 Coffee
Coffee.description: 咖啡是一种受欢迎的热饮，通常由酒馆在清晨提供，为普通人开启新的一天带来所需的能量。其制作过程是将咖啡豆研磨成细粉，然后与水一同放入壶中加热。当混合物开始起泡并沸腾时即可饮用，通常还会根据喜好加入糖或牛奶。

Thousand Yes.name: Thousand Yes
Thousand Yes.description: This book contains 500 pages, with the word "Yes" written repeatedly on both sides of every page. Many believe there is more to this book than what meets the eye, but no one has yet identified its true purpose. Holding the book gives an ominous, unsettling feeling, and staring into its pages for too long makes it seem as if the word itself begins to warp and shift. The only clue to its origin is the name of its author is a small note stating "One."

Common Pandora.name: 红海鲷‌ Common Pandora
Common Pandora.description: 红海鲷‌，又被称为“鲷鱼之王”或红潘多拉，是一种备受推崇的食用鱼。然而，渔船必须驶入深海、面对汹涌海况才能捕获它。尽管捕捞环境艰难，这种鱼本身却相对容易上钩，通常会迅速咬食鱿鱼、蠕虫或虾等天然饵料。

Contract.name: 契约 Contract
Contract.description: 契约是双方之间签订的书面协议，用于明确工作、交易或其他安排的具体内容。它由双方签署，并由第三方加盖印章作为见证。契约中可以包含完成相关事项所需的各类详细条款。通常会制作三份副本，双方各执一份，另有一份作为存档或由见证人保管。

Copper Slag.name: 铜渣 Copper Slag
Copper Slag.description: 在通过冶炼提取铜的过程中，杂质会浮到表面形成铜渣。这种材料已成为一种长期使用的建筑资源，可用于道路铺设，并可作为建筑材料砂浆中砂子的替代品。

Coral.name: 珊瑚 Coral
Coral.description: 粉碎后的珊瑚曾是制作治疗药水的极佳材料，因为它能确保这些治疗药剂具有更高的最低疗效。如今它仅用于制作稀有的红色染料。

Corn.name: 玉米 Corn
Corn.description: 玉米是最常种植的谷物之一，因为它可以被加工成油和面粉。玉米本身是诸界中人类与牲畜的重要主食。这种植物本身称为“Maize”，通常在气候转暖的季节开始种植，因为它不耐寒。

Corvus Corax.name: 渡鸦头骨 Corvus Corax
Corvus Corax.description: Corvus Corax更常被称为渡鸦的头骨。它主要被最迷信的人用作指引护符，或串在链条或绳线上作为饰物悬挂。对另一些人来说，它象征着智慧、聪颖与洞察力。根据不同地区、传统与民俗，它也可以象征死亡，或作为转变与蜕变的象征。

Cow.name: 牛 Cow
Cow.description: 牛被认为是最常被饲养的牲畜之一，对农夫而言极为珍贵，因为它几乎没有任何部分会被浪费。从肉类和乳制品等基础产物，到可制成皮革的兽皮、由皮与骨提炼的胶，再到用于制作餐具、别针、鱼钩、长笛或骨针等工艺品，牛身上的每一部分都会被充分利用。

Cranesbill.name: 老鹳草 Cranesbill
Cranesbill.description: 老鹳草是一种常见的园艺植物，具有多种药用价值。大多数平民会在家中种植它，用来泡制草本茶。老鹳草这种药草可作为漱口剂使用，以缓解口腔溃疡带来的疼痛；也可以煮成热饮，用于舒缓剧烈的胃部不适。

Creamatory.name: 奶油饮 Creamatory
Creamatory.description: 市集上流传着一句话：如果你第一口喝下去后，杯中的奶油能留下完美的螺旋纹路，那么这一年将会顺遂而富足；若是反而凝结成块，那最好别远行，因为厄运正在前方等着你。很久以前，我也曾无视这种迷信，甚至嗤之以鼻，结果却在黑暗的沼泽里折断了马车车轴，被困了好几天，直到修好才能脱身。从那以后，我总会留意奶油的形状。你可以说我愚蠢，但当你亲眼见过奶油只在某个特定的夜晚凝结成那样古怪的形态时，你就会学会对这杯饮品保持敬意。

Crimson Noble Slab.name: 绯红贵族金条 Crimson Noble Slab
Crimson Noble Slab.description: 这些特殊的金条由 100% 纯金制成，重约 2.2 磅，很长一段时间里，它们只被称为贵族金条。随着人们越来越频繁地将这些金条用于邪恶勾当，它们的名称也从贵族金条改成了绯红贵族金条。贵族们往往用这些金条进行支付，因为上面没有家族印记，有助于避免牵扯进任何形式的敌对争斗——从政变、颠覆到暗杀。大多数商人都广泛接受这些金条，并明白它们代表着一笔已经完成的交易。拒收它们，可能会让商人及其所爱之人陷入危险之中。

Crucible Steel.name: 坩埚钢 Crucible Steel
Crucible Steel.description: 当铁匠能够接触到古老魔法时，熔炉便成为了一处神圣之地。在这里，最精良的武器与护甲得以锻造。其中最珍贵的金属便是坩埚钢，它以强度与耐久闻名。久远的时代里，人们曾说坩埚钢是在大地炽热的深处锻成，又经由千颗太阳的热力淬炼。它坚固无比，即使面对最锋利的刀刃，也能始终保持锋锐。最优秀的铁匠往往会花上数小时，甚至数天来锻打坩埚钢，小心翼翼地将其塑造成理想的形态。最终成品便是一件几乎坚不可摧的武器或护甲。

Ticks.name: Ticks
Ticks.description: Ticks are insects that latch onto passing animals and common folk to feed on their blood. They are found in tall grasses and wooded areas, waiting for a host to brush by. Everytime a herbalist has to handle an tick, as they are known carriers of diseases, it asks the farmers to check all livestock that they own as well.

Timer.name: Timer
Timer.description: Usually associated with explosives, for situations where multiple explosions need to happen simultaneously, an amazing piece of engineering, whether for the demolition of a gate wall or a sturdy mine wall. The timer is a necessity for a planned chained reaction. However, it is also used in sporting events to provide a clear indication of a winner.

Timpani.name: Timpani
Timpani.description: Timpani, frequently referred to as kettledrums, are percussion instruments characterized by their striking sound. Played with specialized drumsticks known as mallets, held in pairs. Musicians often have a collection of timpani mallets on hand, as each mallet produces a unique timbre and sound. The variety enhances their role in orchestral music, where they provide a one-of-a-kind rhythmic experience.

Tiretaine.name: Tiretaine
Tiretaine.description: The Tiretaine cloth is made with a mix of wool and other fabrics, mainly used to create towels, rags, and commoner dresses, as it is cheap to manufacture and easy to mass produce.

Cucumber.name: 黄瓜 Cucumber
Cucumber.description: 黄瓜通常是生吃或用于沙拉，在极少数特定菜肴中也会被烹饪食用。也可以将其放入盐、野莳萝和水的混合液或醋中腌制，以便在冬季保存。

Cumin.name: 孜然 Cumin
Cumin.description: 孜然籽因其独特的风味和香气而被用作香料。它可以在一些奶酪（如莱顿奶酪）中找到，也可以在制作面包时加入。这种香料是许多地方菜肴的主料，以其温暖、朴实的味道为菜肴增添风味。

Titanium.name: Titanium
Titanium.description: Titanium is an incredible metal. It is really expensive and rare, but it also has some amazing strengthening properties that make it one of the strongest metals around! When forged into weapons and armor by the most famous blacksmiths throughout the realm, this precious silver-gray metal is said to be near indestructible, not to mention incredibly light yet stronger than steel, making it the perfect material for crafting weapons and armor.

Dark River Gold.name: 暗河金币 Dark River Gold
Dark River Gold.description: 这些来历不明的金币总是由渔夫发现，而它们一旦出现，便被视为不祥之兆。每当它们在河流或海洋中被打捞上来，与附近城市之间的贸易和交流往往会减缓甚至中断，而关于怪物入侵、海啸与火灾的传闻也常常会从该地区传出。这些传闻的来源至今尚未得到证实。然而，尽管这些金币极为稀有，持有者往往也会迅速将其转手，几乎不求利润，或许正是因为人们普遍认为它们带有不祥的关联。

Dark-Bat Remains.name: 暗蝠遗骸 Dark-Bat Remains
Dark-Bat Remains.description: 当夜行生物化作更加兽性的形态时，追猎它们的猎人往往只剩下一种办法来结束狩猎——斩首。如此一来，生物的血肉与皮肤会被灼烧殆尽，只留下头骨。这样的头骨通常会被作为战利品保存，并被极为珍视，因为能从这种生物手中活下来本就是极其罕见的壮举。

Datolite.name: 硅硼钙石 Datolite
Datolite.description: 历史记录表明，硅硼钙石曾一度被用作一种货币形式。然而，由于它容易磨损损坏，人们最终以更耐用、并被普遍认可的金属硬币取而代之。尽管这种宝石依然具有价值，但其作为货币的历史意义已逐渐淡去。

Death Cap.name: 死帽菇 Death Cap
Death Cap.description: 那些最不受欢迎的人最偏爱的蘑菇。食用死帽菇往往致命。在大多数靠近文明地区的森林里，人们都会竖起告示，警告行人不要随意食用野生蘑菇，因为死帽菇几乎可以在任何地方生长。

Deep Blue Spinel.name: 深蓝尖晶石 Deep Blue Spinel
Deep Blue Spinel.description: 一种色泽深蓝偏紫的上等晶体，通常被镶嵌于珠宝首饰之中。

Deep Green Spinel.name: 深绿尖晶石 Deep Green Spinel
Deep Green Spinel.description: 深受森林住民喜爱的这种尖晶石，常被用来装饰护甲与武器，也常用于制作吊坠、戒指或手镯。

Demontoid.name: 翠榴石 Demantoid
Demontoid.description: 翠榴石是最稀有的石榴石种类之一，拥有可与祖母绿媲美的鲜艳色泽，其闪耀光彩甚至胜过钻石。无论是宝石鉴赏家还是珠宝爱好者都对其趋之若鹜，因此翠榴石的售价通常远远高于其地区常规价格。

Detonator.name: 雷管 Detonator
Detonator.description: 这是一种深受矿工青睐的工具，与火药炸药配合使用时，能迅速开凿孔洞或隧道，以抵达地下更有价值的金属矿脉。然而，由于爆炸偶尔会不稳定，且存在弹片损坏矿井支撑结构的罕见风险，钻具往往仍是更受偏好的选择。

Dioptase.name: 翠铜矿 Dioptase
Dioptase.description: 翠铜矿深受宝石收藏家的喜爱，有时也会被切磨成小型宝石用于珠宝首饰。除此之外，这种宝石还可以被研磨成粉末，用作绘画颜料。

Dobsonfly.name: 鱼蛉 Dobsonfly
Dobsonfly.description: 鱼蛉是一种体型很大的昆虫，由一种被称为地狱虫的幼虫发育而来。它以水生昆虫、花蜜、糖水、蠕虫，甚至小鱼为食。虽然其庞大的体型可能令人望而生畏，但鱼蛉约一周的短暂寿命与夜行习性，意味着许多平民可能终其一生都不会遇见一只。

Draconic Staters.name: 龙族斯塔特 Draconic Staters
Draconic Staters.description: 这些硬币在历史上被称为最早使用的货币，历经漫长岁月也几乎没有改动。它们通常由龙的仆从铸造，并总是刻有龙翼或龙首图案。由于巨龙的宝库中往往藏有品质不一的贵金属，这些硬币的含金价值通常低于市场上的其他金币。根据地区不同，人们也可能因其图案而将它们称为“翼币”。

Dragon Scales.name: 龙鳞 Dragon Scales
Dragon Scales.description: 龙鳞并没有太多用途，然而，诸地流传着一些故事：在危急时刻，你可以将龙鳞当作临时防具使用，因为它们相当坚固，形状也类似盾牌，并且能够抵抗其所属巨龙所对应的元素。

Dragon Talons.name: 龙爪 Dragon Talons
Dragon Talons.description: 巨龙爪子的锋锐足以切开最精工锻造的钢铁。考虑到其尺寸，它们有时会被改制成手工武器；根据大小不同，既可以制成坚固锋锐的短剑，也可以制成极为轻盈的长剑。

Dragon Touch.name: 龙吻戒 Dragon Touch
Dragon Touch.description: 龙吻戒指是一件骗局珠宝，被有意包装成补全尊贵套装的失落第三件组件，用来搭配“龙之眼”耳坠与“龙之心”吊坠。它主要由黄铁矿和廉价玛瑙制成，因此确实具有一定价值。然而，一旦这件珠宝被证明并非卖家所宣称的东西，那位巧舌如簧的骗子早已不见踪影。

Toadstool.name: Toadstool
Toadstool.description: It usually has a dotted red cap and is assumed to be poisonous. If eaten it can cause serious illness and on occasion death. Harvested by gatherers and used by alchemists in creating poisons or antidotes.

Tourmaline.name: Tourmaline
Tourmaline.description: It is one of the most prized minerals, and its fine crystals can be among the most beautiful examples in the mineral kingdom. Instead of being faceted, many fine Tourmaline crystals are preserved for their beauty. It is also believed that they can absorb any spell cast upon them, and convert it into lightning.

Toy Horse.name: Toy Horse
Toy Horse.description: The toy horse provides a sense of adventure for young children, crafted primarily from wood and often accompanied by wooden toy swords and shields. It sparks the imagination and is a must for the new aspiring adventurers.

Transport Shackles.name: Transport Shackles
Transport Shackles.description: These shackles, made from iron or steel, like their counterparts and intended for easy mass production, function similar to pillories. They are typically designed to link several sets of shackles in a daisy chain to facilitate the transport of prisoners between locations.

Truffle.name: Truffle
Truffle.description: The truffle is a subterranean mushroom, they are often located with the help of hogs possessing a refined sense of smell. Due to their high price and strong aroma, truffles are used sparingly in gourmet cooking. Supplies are available commercially as unadulterated fresh produce or preserved, typically in a light brine. Since their volatile aromas dissipate quickly when heated, truffles are generally served raw and shaved over warm, simple dishes to highlight their unique flavor.

Tungsten Ingot.name: Tungsten Ingot
Tungsten Ingot.description: Tungsten Ingots are a rare product, crafted only by experienced blacksmiths using some of the hottest smelters. The pure Tungsten Ingot’s primary use lies in alchemical research, particularly in the development of new, powerful acids capable of dissolving everything. Due to their rarity and the immense difficulty in forging them, locating a properly refined one is akin to finding a needle in a haystack.

Tungsten Rod.name: Tungsten Rod
Tungsten Rod.description: Tungsten, sometimes referred to as "Wolfram", is one of the heaviest metals used in any form of crafting. Due to its heavy nature and tough molding, it is primarily used in alloying with softer metals, such as Rhenium or Aluminum all to enhance their durability and shelf life. Another use of tungsten is in the creation of combat battering rams, from which it derives its name.

Turnip.name: Turnip
Turnip.description: Turnips are a favorite for farmers as they do not require a long growing season.  The roots are cooked and served whole or mashed and are used in stews. They can also be used as fodder for cattle.

Undersea Remains.name: Undersea Remains
Undersea Remains.description: These bones are so rare, that nobody can identify them clearly. Some state that they belong to unseen horrors from different realms, or of an ancient form of fish. These are highly valued by most collectors due to their complex retrieval process.

Utility Acid.name: Utility Acid
Utility Acid.description: A regular strong acid, strong enough to corrode iron and bronze, however, not strong enough to dissolve stronger metals. It is usually a must-have for a locksmith, as it is quite efficient against most forms of locks and tends to save some money in the long term, given how regular iron pliers get damaged with repeated use.

Vanadium.name: Vanadium
Vanadium.description: Vanadium is a desired metal throughout the realms, and for very good reason. It is insanely rare and has a deep, green-gray luster that shines in the light. Some blacksmiths and alchemists can imbue vanadium with magical properties, making it even more valuable. Vanadium is very light yet stronger than steel and is speculated that it can absorb and store magical energy.

Vegetable Soup.name: Vegetable Soup
Vegetable Soup.description: Vegetable soups are typically crafted from locally available ingredients. Potatoes, squash, beans, and a variety of herbs such as dill, thyme, parsley, and tomatoes are commonly incorporated, providing a unique taste to each region's variation. In some areas, fruits are also added to lend a more exotic flavor.

Walnut.name: Walnut
Walnut.description: Harvested from the walnut tree, they can be used in several recipes for an added taste. Such as Sprinkled on salads, added to sauces, created dehydrated fruits, added on bakery products, and many more other culinary uses but it also can be eaten raw.

Wardrobe.name: Wardrobe
Wardrobe.description: Due to the considerable amount of materials involved, wardrobes are generally made to order by carpenters in smaller towns or villages.

Watcher Cake.name: Watcher Cake
Watcher Cake.description: Crafted with a topping that's as rare as it is enigmatic, sourced mainly from the quests or missions of adventurers. This cake is rumored to slow down the aging process by half for the year following consumption. However, such rumors have never been confirmed.

Weakfish.name: Weakfish
Weakfish.description: Weakfish are known for their delicious taste and spirited fight. They migrate inshore during the warmer months to spawn and thrive in estuarine and coastal waters. Primarily used in cooking due to its ease of preparation, Weakfish is commonly baked or fried. Alternatively, its delicate texture makes it an excellent option for fish tacos or fish and potato chips.

Watermelon.name: Watermelon
Watermelon.description: Watermelon is a sweet, commonly consumed summer fruit, usually, as fresh slices, diced in mixed fruit salads, or as juice. Watermelon juice can be blended with other fruit juices or made into wine.

Where to Find Veins - A Prospector's Gui.name: Where to Find Veins - A Prospector's Guide to Ore Finding
Where to Find Veins - A Prospector's Gui.description: Detailed guide on the versatility of minerals and how they absorb properties from their surroundings. The book provides extensive information on how to quickly identify minerals by color once found in a cave or mine system. It includes details such as how hematite, while considered a lesser gem, can be processed to extract iron ore, and how bauxite can similarly be refined to produce aluminum. Miners, alchemists, and artisans alike also review this guide to refine their understanding of valuable mineral resources and their practical applications and composition.

Wild Mint.name: Wild Mint
Wild Mint.description: The leaves are a popular choice to garnish some beverages and desserts. Gatherers farm huge quantities of Wild Mint as they can also be made into teas to treat colds and to aid digestion.

Wooden Bowl.name: Wooden Bowl
Wooden Bowl.description: Carved from hardwoods, wooden bowls are both aesthetically pleasing and practical, known for their heat retention and easy handling. Nevertheless, their porous nature can complicate cleaning and maintenance, necessitating special care.

Wooden Ladle.name: Wooden Ladle
Wooden Ladle.description: The ladle plays a crucial role in most kitchens. Wooden ladles are specifically used with expensive or cast-iron pots to prevent scratching and extend their lifespan.

Wooden Ornamentation.name: Wooden Ornamentation
Wooden Ornamentation.description: In some regions, common folk enjoy extensive ornamentation on their houses, ships, and even fences. Though it tends to be an expensive project, the end result is visually appealing for generations to come.
Wooden Ornamentation.gmnote: Alternative icons exist in the module's folder

Worm.name: Worm
Worm.description: Worms are an common sight in healthy soil, mainly after heavy rains. They consume decaying roots making them a good sight, and a hope of good harvest, in any garden or farm. They are also widely used by commoners and fishermen as reliable bait for fishing.

Zinc.name: Zinc
Zinc.description: Zinc is a rare, shimmering metal that is known for its ability to protect against the elements. It has a soft, silver color that glimmers in the light.  It is often used to coat other metals, such as iron or steel, to protect them from rust and decay. In ancient times, it was believed to have magical properties and was used in the crafting of powerful talismans and amulets, even magical mirrors.

Zither.name: Zither
Zither.description: The zither is an instrument that exists in various shapes and sizes, with the defining characteristic of being a plucked instrument. Numerous regional variations can be found worldwide. In some cultures, the zither is celebrated as an all-rounded instrument, valued for its beautiful and resonant sound. This instrument is not only a staple in music but is also well-regarded in some religious settings, where its beautiful sound enhances choir performances.

Zoisite.name: Zoisite
Zoisite.description: This particular gem can be found in a variety of colors, including green, brown, pink, and black. These gems are particularly favored by people who lead secluded lives, as they are believed to ward off negative thoughts and emotions, whether they are caused by magic or other means. They are said to promote a sense of calm when worn. These gems are in high demand among hermits, monks, and common folk who live in remote areas.

Zombie.name: Zombie
Zombie.description: Zombie is an exotic cocktail made of fruit juices, such as lime and grapefruit juice, cynnamon syrup, liqueurs, and three different types of rum. Usually served with catchy frazes like "Let's make the dead walk and talk." or "Craving action? Soon you’ll be staring through the eyes of the undead!"

Zucchini.name: Zucchini
Zucchini.description: Zucchini is usually served cooked and can be prepared using a variety of techniques. These include steaming, boiling, grilling, stuffing and baking, barbecuing, frying, or incorporating it into soufflés and stir-fries.

Dreamfreezer's Draft.name: 冻梦药剂 Dreamfreezer's Draft
Dreamfreezer's Draft.description: 这种药剂通常由牧师、草药师或各类医者使用，用于取出皮下寄生的小生物，或协助进行危及生命的手术。药剂会使服用者进入类似昏迷的状态；在这种状态下，人们相信被遗忘的神祇与现世神祇会前来闲谈，然而这一说法从未得到任何程度上的准确证实。这种药剂的缺点在于，不同服用者对它的抗性各不相同，而一旦手术开始，负责该状况的主治医者就必须迅速行动，因为药效持续时间没有固定且明确的范围。根据服用者不同，它可能只持续数分钟，也可能持续数小时。森林住民对这种药剂完全免疫。

Durian.name: 榴莲 Durian
Durian.description: 榴莲果实外壳坚硬，呈圆形，绿色且布满尖刺，内部则是柔软可食的果肉。在大多数热带地区，一旦气温下降，就必须尽快采收，因为果实会突然停止生长。尽管它被认为是最美味的水果之一，但它的气味却让大多数平民望而却步，其味道常被拿来与臭鼬喷出的气味或未经处理的污水相提并论。

Emerald.name: 绿宝石 Emerald
Emerald.description: 半透明的绿宝石会被切割并打磨成宝石饰品，主要作为礼物赠予王室或贵族。过去，人们将其与健康、生育和成长联系在一起，并常用来装饰能够增进治疗技艺的魔法物品。

Epidote.name: 绿帘石 Epidote
Epidote.description: 绿帘石是一种在各类矿井中相当常见的装饰性石材，因其兼具多种用途而备受珍视。它尤其常被用作驱逐不死生物药剂的材料，也可研磨制成用于书写卷轴的墨料。

Equipment Order.name: 装备调令 Equipment Order
Equipment Order.description: 这是一份正式法令，用于征调大量装备，其中包括护甲与武器，通常作为备战或支援正在进行中的战争之准备。作为回应，铁匠们会提交一份材料清单，估算完成这项需求所需的各类物资数量。

Evernight.name: 永夜 Evernight
Evernight.description: 永夜被视为收藏家之酿，部分原因在于其神秘的特性。据说，当它被饮下后，另一瓶永夜会出现在诸界某处、某个随机架子上的其他饮品之间。历经无数年代，炼金术士、草药师与酿酒师都曾尝试复刻它，却无一成功。它独特的性质与未知的原料，使其真正变得无价。有一则经久不衰的传闻声称，若将其与晨风和纯昼混合，便能获得不朽。然而，由于它会自行消失，从未有人成功将三者合为一剂。

Expensive Fish & Where to Get Them.name: 昂贵的鱼与它们的出处 Expensive Fish & Where to Get Them
Expensive Fish & Where to Get Them.description: 这本书很快便深入介绍了各种钓鱼区域的细节；然而，其中大量信息看起来并不完整，甚至具有误导性。它常常在酒馆里由自称知名渔夫的人兜售，通常以不熟练的平民为目标，作为一种一夜暴富骗局的一部分。其核心理念很简单：只要前往最近的水域，抓到什么就卖什么，从中获利。整本书反复使用一句宣传语来吸引读者：“一次收获，就能改变你的人生。方法就在这里！”

Exploding Stick.name: 爆破棒 Exploding Stick
Exploding Stick.description: 爆破棒通常因其钻凿用途而得名，用于在坚硬厚实的岩石或矿石上制造细小裂缝，以便之后再用镐子或钻具继续开凿。通常会有专门的技师使用最优质的火药来制作并安装它们。它们不会造成巨大的爆炸声势，但在清理岩石方面却十分有效。

Faction Earrings.name: 阵营耳坠 Faction Earrings
Faction Earrings.description: 作为一种象征性礼节，当加入某些特定且声名卓著的派系时，成员会收到一枚戒指或一对耳坠作为欢迎礼物，同时还会获赠带有相同标志的钢笔、笔记本等其他随身物品。这有助于快速识别成员，也能在最初营造出一种归属感。

Fangs Necklace.name: 獠牙项链 Fangs Necklace
Fangs Necklace.description: 啊，獠牙项链。每年就在红收节篝火点燃之前，从港湾镇到刺棘丘陵集市的珠宝匠们，都会开始成打地切磨这种饰品。它主要是一件富有戏剧感的装饰品。尖锐而形似牙齿的坠饰悬挂在金属丝或链条上，通常由红色玻璃或烟晶制成。有些宝石大师会将真正的石榴石切磨成尖牙状，但你在市场摊位上看到的那些闪闪发亮之物，大多只是彩色玻璃的巧妙工艺。佩戴者立于火把光下时，这些坠饰会映出一抹红光，看起来令人不安地像新鲜血液，其目的正是模拟夜之獠牙。

Figworts.name: 玄参 Figworts
Figworts.description: 玄参是一种备受追捧的草药，许多草药师都会寻找它，因为它可用于治疗皮肤炎症、缓解疼痛，甚至可作为温和的泻药。大多数玄参通常生长在肥沃的林地中，因此采集者进入森林时，都会尽量留意它们的踪迹，因为草药师往往愿意为其支付高价。

Finger Claw.name: 指爪 Finger Claw
Finger Claw.description: 它们并非武器，尽管有些爱出风头的年轻小子喜欢戴着它们在空中乱抓。说到底，它们只是戏服配件，如此简单，却被人们以一种奇异的严肃态度对待。在河地，人们会把染着浆果汁的指爪分发给孩子，用来标志季节更替；而在港口城镇，你会见到镶嵌彩色玻璃碎片的指爪，它们以高价卖给来访商人，好让这些人融入那些古怪贵族的圈子。

Fly Agaric.name: 毒蝇伞 Fly Agaric
Fly Agaric.description: 毒蝇伞因其毒性，在艰难时期有时也被称作“墓园守望者”。然而，即便有警告，仍有人会食用它，因为人们相信它能够与神祇建立联系，而结果往往致命。这种蘑菇最主要的用途是作为杀虫剂——当它与牛奶混合时，既能吸引苍蝇，也能将其杀死，这也正是它名字的由来。

Fly potion.name: 飞行药剂 Fly Potion
Fly potion.description: 与普遍认知相反，这种药剂并不会赋予饮用者飞行能力。它其实是其真正能够提供飞行能力的对应药剂在调配过程中产生的一种略显失衡的副产物。此药剂真正的效果，是让使用者暂时能够在 400 英尺范围内清晰看见远处细节。不过，使用者必须专注于特定位置，并需要一段适应过程，就像眼睛在黑暗中逐渐适应一样。它能够让远处景象变得更加清晰，但并不会提升任何感知能力。

Fruit Puree.name: 水果泥 Fruit Puree
Fruit Puree.description: 这种甜点可以使用市场上各种常见水果来制作，例如桃子、苹果和草莓。首先将水果去皮，并去除果核与种子，然后将其煮至足够柔软，便于捣成泥。为了增添风味，人们常会在其中加入柠檬汁或橙汁。这道甜点通常作为大多数烘焙食品的配菜，因为它与这些食物搭配得十分完美。或者，它也可以冷藏后食用，用来对抗炎热的夏日。

Fruit Vulcano Lava Pie.name: 水果火山熔岩派 Fruit Vulcano Lava Pie
Fruit Vulcano Lava Pie.description: 从第一口到最后一口，这种派都令人愉悦无比。当你用勺子戳破酥皮时，滚烫的果酱会缓缓流出，宛如熔岩一般，这也正是它名字的由来。

Fustian.name: 粗斜纹布 Fustian
Fustian.description: 一种厚重的织物，用于制作衣物，通常被用作服装的衬垫，以应对寒冷季节，或用于建筑等重体力劳动时穿着的耐用服装。

Gabbro.name: 辉长岩 Gabbro
Gabbro.description: 辉长岩在建筑术语中被称为黑花岗岩，因其坚固耐用，主要用于建造石栅栏，尽管加工起来颇为困难。人们开采它，主要是为了获取同一岩石中所含的其他矿物，包括铬、镍、钴、铜、金、银和铂。

Gallows.description: 一根直立木柱与顶部横梁构成了绞刑架的基本结构，用于处决犯下骇人罪行的死囚。除了惩罚之外，它也曾作为公开展示正义的工具；在某些情况下，动乱之后，它还会在地区上层阶级的授意下，成为对民众的警告。
Gallows.name: 绞刑架 Gallows

Garden Hoe.name: 园艺锄 Garden Hoe
Garden Hoe.description: 园艺锄用途多样，既可通过开出浅沟来帮助播种，也能协助清除杂草。由于锄刃锋利而宽阔，农夫们喜欢用园艺锄成排割除杂草。它能轻松滑入土壤表层之下，切断恼人杂草的根部。

Garlic.name: 大蒜 Garlic
Garlic.description: 大蒜是绝大多数菜肴中最常用的香辛料。无论是煎炒、切片、切丁、剁碎，甚至水煮，只要是一道熟食，其中很可能就会加入少许大蒜。

Gauze.name: 纱布 Gauze
Gauze.description: 纱布主要由亚麻制成，主要用途是清洁伤口。为了增强效果，人们会将蜂蜜或醋等防腐材料与这类布料一同用于包扎，有时还会加入药草以加快愈合过程。大多数旅行者都会随身携带几块预先备好的纱布。

Gear.name: 齿轮 Gear
Gear.description: 一种普通的齿轮零件，适用于所有机械装置之中，从时钟到门锁再到压力阀门，都是各种机械物件与结构中不可或缺的专用部件。没有它，这些装置便无法存在。它主要由铜、黄铜或青铜制成，能够防锈，并可持续使用数十年。

Glazed Fruits.name: 蜜釉水果 Glazed Fruits
Glazed Fruits.description: 这种美味甜点所选用的水果会有所不同，取决于面包坊常去的早市上能买到什么；在少数罕见情况下，甚至会使用由魔法召唤出的水果。虽然任何新鲜水果都可以使用，但苹果因易于烘烤而最为常见。不过，面包师使用其他水果也并不稀奇，例如橙子、葡萄，甚至野生甜浆果，以增添额外的风味。水果烤好后，面包师会小心地涂上一层由蜂蜜与优质果酱调成的糖釉，若顾客要求，也可使用价格更高的巧克力；其目标客群从年幼孩童到嗜甜的成年人皆有。

Gleaming Unicorn.name: 闪耀硬币 Gleaming Unicorn
Gleaming Unicorn.description: 当森林住民试图建立新的林地聚落时，他们会使用一种原初魔法，将地下的所有金属抽取出来，并以挖出的贵金属铸造成硬币。这些硬币是铂与银的合金，因此相当贵重，也会在他们进入文明地区时用于贸易。其价值大约相当于铂金的 25%。

Gneiss.name: 片麻岩 Gneiss
Gneiss.description: 片麻岩作为建筑材料有着多种用途，例如地板、装饰石材、墓碑、建筑外墙饰面石以及工作台面。根据其产地不同，它可能呈现出美丽的天然纹理，因此成为装饰性建筑中最受追捧的材料之一。

God's Wrath.name: 神之怒 God's Wrath
God's Wrath.description: 它有时被称为硫磺石，更常见的名字则是硫磺。炼金术士通常使用块状硫磺来制造能够腐蚀万物的强酸。它也常常成为炼金术团体与宗教团体争执的中心，因为某些宗教认为它是神圣之物——它燃烧时会呈现独特的火焰颜色，焰色是一种浓郁的紫色。然而炼金术士则强烈建议不要直接燃烧这些硫磺块，因为他们认为这样做会对健康造成无法挽回的损害。草药师则会利用它来制作多种绿色染料。

Gold Bangle.name: 金手镯 Gold Bangle
Gold Bangle.description: 金手镯是一种由纯金打造的手镯，象征着最高阶层的贵族身份与社会地位的巅峰。它先由技艺精湛的铁匠通过精密模具锻造成型，再交由著名的金匠进行细致打磨，并饰以华丽繁复的纹样。这类手镯通常价格高昂，因此也常成为盗贼与骗子觊觎的目标。

Gold Ingot.name: 金锭 Gold Ingot
Gold Ingot.description: 金锭是商人与贵族之间最常见的货币形式，因其纯度高且便于运输而备受重视。珠宝匠同样青睐它，因为这种金属没有杂质，非常适合用来制作镶嵌宝石的戒环、手镯或项链。一些王国甚至会使用金锭来巩固条约、资助军事行动，以确保军队装备精良并保持忠诚。

Goldilocks.name: 金发姑娘 Goldilocks
Goldilocks.description: 金发姑娘以明亮的果香与柔和的麦芽底韵交织出的和谐滋味，牢牢俘获味蕾。每一口都能展现出层层递进的风味，吸引那些追求深度与细腻变化，而非简单熟悉口感的啤酒爱好者。

Gong.name: 锣 Gong
Gong.description: 锣是一种扁平的圆形金属盘，通常以槌敲击发声。它由青铜、黄铜或铁制成，并悬挂在支架上。锣的尺寸各异，从小型到大型皆有，既可以预先调音，也可能需要手动调音。作为乐器，锣有多种用途，从用于歌剧演出，到在各种场合充当信号装置，例如用于格斗、竞赛等活动，甚至作为警报。

Good Luck Potion No. 12.name: 好运药剂十二号 Good Luck Potion No. 12
Good Luck Potion No. 12.description: 据说只需一小口，便能让人变得相当幸运的药剂配方。虽然这种药剂的实际效果仍有争议，但该配方已经历过十一次不同修订。它在贵族之间颇受欢迎。

Gooseberry.name: 醋栗 Gooseberry
Gooseberry.description: 醋栗植株虽然深受野生动物喜爱，但它既是重要的食物来源，也可能成为疾病传播的媒介。它的浆果为许多动物提供了必要的营养，包括鸟类和熊，它们在夏季常依赖这些果实维持生存。然而，醋栗传播迅速，也因此常被视为对当地植物群的入侵威胁，尤其是松树等针叶树，它们可能因醋栗携带的疱锈菌而遭受长期损害。

Grandfather Clock.name: 落地钟 Grandfather Clock
Grandfather Clock.description: 落地钟，也被称为长箱钟、高箱钟、厅堂钟或座钟，是一种高大的独立式重锤驱动摆钟，其钟摆置于钟壳的塔身或腰部之内。这类钟通常由最有经验的木匠打造，并由专门的工程师协助完成。

Grapes.name: 葡萄 Grapes
Grapes.description: 大部分葡萄产量都用于酿酒，几乎所有酒馆或旅店都会与葡萄园签订长期供应契约。葡萄有多种颜色，例如深红、黑色、深蓝、黄色、绿色、橙色、粉色和紫色。它们也可用于制作果酱，或晒干制成葡萄干，再用于烹饪，最常见的是各类糕点之中。

Gravestone.name: 墓碑 Gravestone
Gravestone.description: 说到红收节的性质，墓碑奇怪地并不只是装饰品。我发现，凡是立有这些墓碑标记的地方，其下方的土地总会有种奇特的骚动感，仿佛正在呼唤着什么。巴考莱泰亚区不止一位看墓人发誓，廉价的石膏墓碑和真正的墓碑一样，都有可能藏着一具不得安宁的尸体。有些学者则认为这全是巧合，或者只是红收节引出了无数战争后残留在城市中的死灵残响。

Great Forest Rolls.name: 大森林卷 Great Forest Rolls
Great Forest Rolls.description: 这种卷饼内馅由新鲜卷心菜、鲜香蘑菇与芳香莳萝混合而成，而面皮则巧妙搭配了酥脆的盐渍杏仁，以增添口感与风味。此外，对于偏好甜味变化的人，也有另一种版本：以内馅为蜜露甜瓜、清爽黄瓜，以及芳香的罗勒与薄荷叶，并将面皮与杏仁刷上甜蜜蜂蜜，使风味更加丰富。

Green Grapes.name: 青葡萄 Green Grapes
Green Grapes.description: 这种水果因味道甜美，且可以整颗食用、不易弄脏手而成为颇受欢迎的小吃。葡萄园会选用最高品质的青葡萄，酿造最纯净上乘的白葡萄酒。

Green Apple.name: 青苹果 Green Apple
Green Apple.description: 旅店老板会用青苹果装饰部分饮品，也会将其作为健康的甜点选择提供给客人。诸界各地许多著名苹果酒都以青苹果酿成，因此旅店与酒馆老板对青苹果的需求一直很高。

Green Breeze Ear Piece.name: 绿风耳饰 Green Breeze Ear Piece
Green Breeze Ear Piece.description: 这件镶有祖母绿宝石的耳饰，专为那些希望彰显自身上层阶级地位的人而设计。它是一件极其昂贵的时尚配饰，尽管许多人都觉得佩戴起来并不舒适。此饰品必须固定在耳朵的特定部位；若佩戴者的耳形无法适配其特殊设计，甚至可能带来明显疼痛。

Green Shadow.name: 绿影药剂 Green Shadow
Green Shadow.description: 这是一种因错误混合任何具有隐形效果的药剂而产生的失败产物。使用后，皮肤会开始呈现类似变色龙般的特性，逐渐改变颜色以匹配周围环境。然而，这种效果生效得相当缓慢，需要一段时间，你才能真正与背景“融为一体”。

Green Soldier Bug.name: 绿兵蝽 Green Soldier Bug
Green Soldier Bug.description: 绿兵蝽，通常更常被称作臭虫，在被压碎时会散发出难闻的气味，并对茄子和番茄等作物构成严重威胁。幸运的是，与绿夏甲虫不同，这种小害虫可以通过鸡和鸭有效控制数量，而不会对作物造成额外损害。

Green Summer Beetle.name: 绿夏甲虫 Green Summer Beetle
Green Summer Beetle.description: 绿夏甲虫在生长季节会成为问题，危害花园和农作物。这些甲虫主要袭击成熟或正在发酵的水果，如无花果、葡萄和浆果。每年夏天，许多平民会在种植过程中加入艾蒿，因为它通常能够起到驱虫作用。

Grilled Ribs.name: 烤排骨 Grilled Ribs
Grilled Ribs.description: 入口即脱骨的烤排骨，裹满香甜微酸的自制酱汁，是大多数酒馆中深受喜爱的招牌菜，常在庆典或特殊活动时供应，也最受那些完成任务凯旋归来的冒险者们欢迎。

Grinder.name: 研磨器 Grinder
Grinder.description: 这是一种木制装置，内部配有精工打造且坚固耐用的铁制齿轮，用于将材料研磨成细粉。其曲柄设计便于操作。虽然它最初是作为传统研钵与研杵的替代品而出现，如今则主要被酒馆用来快速研磨干胡椒。

Grog.name: 朗姆水 Grog
Grog.description: 朗姆水基本上是用水稀释的朗姆酒。在远征新发现的土地时，船长会将朗姆酒桶加水，以便让船员保持满足，同时将有限的储备延长使用时间。这种做法有助于在新鲜食材稀缺的长途航行中维持高昂士气。

Hair Pin.name: 发簪 Hair Pin
Hair Pin.description: 这是一种以小型宝石点缀的精致发簪，用以象征高贵身份。它的设计方式使其在固定发髻时能够展现优雅与风度，或单纯作为上层贵族阶级身份的标志。这类发簪主要由经验丰富的珠宝匠打造，并且通常只接受价格极高的定制委托。

Hambergite.name: 硼铍石 Hambergite
Hambergite.description: 硼铍石通常为无色或带有淡淡的蓝色。尽管它被归类为半宝石，但其价值更多体现在照明用途上。它常被用于灯具之中，用来增强蜡烛及其他光源的亮度，无论那些光源是常规光源，还是由奥术手段产生。

Hazelnut.name: Hazelnut
Hazelnut.description: Hazelnuts get their name from their color, hazel. Sometimes they are also referred to as cobnuts or filberts. The nuts are used in cakes or as crumble toppings, however, if grounded they can also be used as flour for baking.

Heart.name: Heart
Heart.description: Looks like a stage prop, doesn’t it? Too shiny, too perfect, the sort of thing you’d expect dangling from a street conjurer’s sleeve. Yet hold one in your palm and you’ll swear it thrums against your skin, slow, steady, like the echo of your own chest. I’ve seen hardened mercenaries drop the thing lika a curse, as if they’d grabbed a live rat.

Hellgrammite.name: Hellgrammite
Hellgrammite.description: Hellgrammites are the perfect fishing bait across the realms, known to attract a wide range of fish including panfish, perch, pickerel, channel catfish, and especially bass. When fishing in deeper water or near heavy structures like submerged logs, tree roots, large rocks, or boat docks, they are thought to be particularly successful at drawing in the big catches, however, quite intimidating to look at.

Henbane.name: Henbane
Henbane.description: The Henbane, also known as the Witches Plant, is a highly toxic plant, similar to the nightshade, however, herbalists use it, in combination with a plethora of other plants, to create anesthetic for sale for any medical practitioners. The Henbane is also a ritualistic herb, being used by magicians and healers in ritual purifications by smoke as well as in the preparation of concoctions, love philters, and 'flying ointments'.

Herring.name: Herring
Herring.description: Herrings can be fished in a variety of ways, from setting bait directly in the water and using nets to capture them in large batches, to more traditional rod fishing. Once caught, herrings are stored in numerous ways, with regional recipes that include raw, fermented, pickled, or salted varieties, as well as smoked or preserved through other methods.

Hexagonite.name: Hexagonite
Hexagonite.description: Hexagonite is an interesting gemstone, distinguished by its hexagonal shape when unearthed, which inspired its name. These gems are often sewn into clothing for social events, symbolically representing eligibility for marriage, particularly for bachelors.

Honey.name: Honey
Honey.description: Honey, the sweet and viscous substance produced by honey bees, is commonly cultivated by many monasteries. Stored within the hive, honey bees construct honeycomb structures made from wax to contain the honey. Additionally, beeswax candles, derived from the honeycomb, are frequently used for lighting homes.

Honey Cookies.name: Honey Cookies
Honey Cookies.description: These types of cookies, made with honey, tend to have a fluffier texture and sweeter taste. Due to their affordability, filling nature, and extended shelf life, most taverns keep them in stock for sale at all times.

Horseshoe.name: Horseshoe
Horseshoe.description: In certain regions, horseshoes are seen as a harbinger of good luck, while in others, they symbolize advanced industry. Typically crafted from steel, the choice of material may vary depending on metal availability in specific areas, including options like bronze, copper, or iron. In exceptionally uncommon cases, materials such as hide, leather, or even wood are considered.

Horsetail.name: Horsetail
Horsetail.description: Horsetail is a common herb that has been used by herbalists for centuries to treat a variety of ailments, including bleeding, wounds, colds, and hay fever. It grows in many parts of the world and is considered poisonous to sheep and cattle, although it is safe for the common folk to consume in small amounts.

Hugger of Faces.name: Hugger of Faces
Hugger of Faces.description: According to some rare studies, the creature’s body wraps tightly to the face, while its tail coils around the neck, to an unknown purpose. However, due to limited documentation of them, their purpose remains uncertain. No living specimens have ever been discovered, only the ocassional exoskeleton shells. Analysis of these shells has led to the theory that the creature's blood is highly acidic as it is capable of dissolving most substances.

Hurdy-Gurdy.name: Hurdy-Gurdy
Hurdy-Gurdy.description: The hurdy-gurdy is a string instrument that creates sound by using a hand-crank-turned, round rosined wheel that rubs against the strings. Melodies are produced on a keyboard, that presses tangents against one or more strings to alter their pitch. This unique mechanism allows for a continuous melody while notes are played, giving the instrument its characteristic sound.

Hyacinth.name: Hyacinth
Hyacinth.description: Hyacinth, alternatively called Jacinth or Flamegem depending on where it's found, is a rare gem prized for its vibrant, fiery orange color. Known for its hardness and durability akin to sapphire and ruby, its unique affinity for fire lends its significance to arcane rituals and applications.

Hyalite.name: Hyalite
Hyalite.description: Hyalite, a milky form of quartz, is mainly utilized for crafting crystal balls. It is particularly favored by novice practitioners of arcane arts and mystical disciplines, leading schools to procure hyalite in large quantities.

Hypersthene.name: Hypersthene
Hypersthene.description: This semi-precious stone displays hues ranging from green, red, and blue, to silver, and is notable for its complete opacity, a rarity among gemstones. Due to its unique characteristics, it fetches a premium price and is primarily utilized to embellish both furniture and clothing.

Idicolite.name: Idicolite
Idicolite.description: Often mistaken for sapphire, this gemstone is actually a variant of tourmaline. Despite attempts by unscrupulous merchants to pass it off as the more valuable sapphire, its discernible, slightly less vibrant color serves as a telltale sign to those with a trained eye.

Invitation.name: Invitation
Invitation.description: Invitations are often used for weddings, official events, or to gain access to the count's court. They vary greatly in style, ranging from highly formal to personal, and from luxurious to official. It’s typically difficult to know exactly what kind of event awaits until the invitation is opened.

Iol.name: Iol
Iol.description: A violet color stone, sometimes referred to as Iolite. Is usually cut into gemstone facets and is used in all types of jewelry, especially rings.

Iris Agate.name: Iris Agate
Iris Agate.description: This stone is a finely-banded and usually colorless agate, however, when cut properly, it displays an amazing show of colors on its surface, sometimes also referred to as "Rainbow Agate"

Jargoon.name: Jargoon
Jargoon.description: Jargoons are highly valued for their deep red color and radiant shine, resembling rubies. There are rumors that this specific gem can either project arcane projectiles or prevent creatures of the night from shifting out of their human form, but further research is required to confirm these claims.

Jasper.name: Jasper
Jasper.description: Each Jasper gemstone is assumed to be unique, as every gemstone has singular pattern, ranging from spotted patterns to ringed, or stripped. In days past it was regarded as a valuable stone, now is mainly used by alchemists and herbalists to create antidotes and magical poisons, because the stones proprieties assist in neutralizing and protecting from poison.

Jewelry box.name: Jewelry box
Jewelry box.description: Jewelry boxes are essential in protecting delicate pieces from damage. While diamonds, being the hardest natural material, can scratch softer gemstones like opals and pearls, they can also mark softer metals like gold. But in general, the regular jewelry boxes provide a dedicated space for jewelry in a centralized storage, reducing the chances of misplacement or loss.

Kiwifruit.name: Kiwifruit
Kiwifruit.description: Often shortened to Kiwi, a well sought after fruit. It can be eaten raw, made into juices, used in baked goods, fruit salads, prepared with meat or used as a garnish.

Kompot.name: Kompot
Kompot.description: Also more commonly known as stewed fruit, this is a very simple recipe that involves only water, sugar, and fruit. Everything is thrown into a pot, boiled, bottled, and served cold. This beverage is made by inns and bakeries alike, using any leftover fruit they have, as the concoction has a very long shelf life when completed.

Ladder.name: Ladder
Ladder.description: The ladder is a simple yet essential tool for reaching elevated areas. Whether in libraries, homes, farms, barns, or used by firefighters to rescue people from high places, ladders are used in various environments for essential tasks.

Kyanite.name: Kyanite
Kyanite.description: Kyanite is generally used in the creation of ceramic dishware, mainly due to its distinctive beautiful blue colors. It is a gemstone that blends easily with many other materials under heat. It can also be ground into a fine powder to make high-quality inks.

Ladybug.description: Ladybugs, associated with luck due to their spots, always follow a plentiful harvest. Common folk love them because they eat aphids and other plant pests, keeping the crops safe. The kids love to look at them and have a small song to accompany it, used to count the spots on the ladybug, believing they bring them long lasting luck.
Ladybug.name: Ladybug

Lead Ingot.name: Lead Ingot
Lead Ingot.description: Through the realms, the lead ingot is a valuable commodity. Skilled craftsmen seek it out to make powerful bullets for their weapons. These bullets are then powered by magic so they can inflict a lot of damage on magical beasts or incorporeal beings. They are also used by alchemists to create potions and elixirs. The lead ingot is very valuable and versatile.

Lemon.name: Lemon
Lemon.description: The applications of regular lemons are close to limitless. They are used as cleaning products as they help with degreasing. In cooking they are used as an ingredient to sour foods for a bit of extra tang. One of the most famous pies is the Lemon pie. They are used in concoctions for skincare and sometimes local cures to help with different ailments. They can often be found in refreshing drinks

Letter From a Loved One.name: Letter From a Loved One
Letter From a Loved One.description:
Hello dear,

We are happy to hear you are doing well, and the last coin you send back we finally we were able to fix the hole we had in the thatch in our kitchen.

The youngest finally came of age to be sent to the abbott to learn to read and write, and how to do so efficently, but our oldest, his eyes are getting darker as time goes, now they are like the deep sea, a deep blue that looks unnatural. If you look long enough you see things... Horrible things.

I am scared... When are you coming back? Please let it be soon.

With love,

Your Dandelion

Liquid Malachite.name: Liquid Malachite
Liquid Malachite.description: Liquid Malachite is generally stored in a wooden container. When taken out of its container and coming in contact with metal, it quickly spreads, attaches, and hardens, but only temporarily. Historically, it was primarily utilized by militaristic tribal communities, as they didn't typically wear armor but rather used a thin woven metal cloth. However, its potential should not be underestimated, as even when combined with tin, the liquid malachite increases the strength and durability of materials to a level comparable to steel.

Liquid Solid Slate.name: Liquid Solid Slate
Liquid Solid Slate.description: Little is known about the process of liquefaction, however, when used as an alloy with another metal, it is said to be unbreakable. When struck by a weapon, an impenetrable shock-wave is created, pushing the weapon back. Due to its weight, it makes it difficult for people trying to move stealthily.

Living Book.description: The fable goes that it is a journal of sorts, said to be owned by a forgotten by time witch. At every solstice, the pages get erased, and new entries can be added. When new entries are added, and if specifically written as a journal, the book itself rolls its eyes as soon as something embarrassing is written in it.
Living Book.name: Living Book

Lover's Embrace.name: Lover's Embrace
Lover's Embrace.description: A couple of sweet gelatin tarts are usually made by boiling bones, removing them from the resulting liquid, and allowing it to cool, making it a gelatin like substance. The gelatin is then flavored with a mixture of fruits, citrus, and sugar until the taste from the bone residue dissipates. Due to its very sweet nature, it is only served as a treat for anniversaries or for newlywed couples.

MIlk.name: MIlk
MIlk.description: Milk is a husbandry byproduct which usually comes from cows, goats, and sheep. It is a white, nutritious liquid often consumed as a beverage or used to make a variety of dairy products such as cheese, butter, and yogurt. Scholars believe it has magical properties that can strengthen bones to an ungodly degree.

Macaw.name: Macaw
Macaw.description: The Macaw, one of the rarer types of parrots, is highly favored by seafaring rogues. These birds keep a watchful eye on the crew, relay commands, and update the captain on the ship's activities while he rests.

Mackerel.name: Mackerel
Mackerel.description: Mackerel can be enjoyed raw, oven-cooked, fried, smoked, or salted with vinegar. Taverns often provide a variety of cooking options to cater to different clientele tastes.

Maidenhair.name: Maidenhair
Maidenhair.description: This herbal supplement is frequently recommended by herbalists and is mainly used to address blood disorders. It is also renowned for its ability to help keep your memory sharp.

Malakim's Touch.name: Malakim's Touch
Malakim's Touch.description: Malakim's Touch is a cocktail made with a blend of multiple types of liquor, quite sweet in nature, it tempts drinkers into having more than they should. Only for them to find themselves the next morning, groaning in pain and begging the heavens and hells for mercy. It usually bears a red color due to the cherry and strawberry liquor that are used in high quantities.

Mallet.name: Mallet
Mallet.description: Wooden mallets are a staple in carpentry, serving to knock wooden pieces together, drive dowels, manipulate chisels, and apply pressure on joints. Notably, they stand apart from most metal hammers by preventing the deformation of the striking end of a metal tool.

Manganese Chips.name: Manganese Chips
Manganese Chips.description: Manganese Chips, after being mined, are rarely used by alchemists or herbalists in the production of potions or medicine, as well as in the creation of herbicides and brown paint. Their primary use is in the forging of a variety of important alloys to make them more resilient.

Marble.name: Marble
Marble.description: The favorite tool of skilled sculptors, marble, transforms thoughts and emotions into beautiful sculptures as they carve it. Polished to a shine, it earns the sculptor a hefty profit when sold. Marble is one of the heaviest and most resilient stones that can be bought with coin. Its elegance makes it a popular choice for nobility, who use it for indoor flooring and heavy furnishings.

Mashed Potatoes.name: Mashed Potatoes
Mashed Potatoes.description: Mashed potatoes represent a classic staple in many cuisines. The process involves boiling potatoes and then mashing them into a soft paste often served as a side dish alongside various meats.

Mason Hammer.name: Mason Hammer
Mason Hammer.description: Commonly called brick hammers, mason's hammers are primarily designed for the precise breaking of bricks, rocks, pavers, or driving stakes. They feature one flat traditional face and, on the back, a short or long chisel-shaped blade. This design allows them to effectively chip off edges or small pieces of stone and cut brick masonry units without the need for a separate chisel.

Mead.name: Mead
Mead.description: Most monasteries keep up the traditions of mead making as a byproduct of beekeeping, especially in areas where grapes could not be grown, however, as long there's access to any form of honey, mead can be easily made. Most taverns simply import honey and begin the fermentation process to produce mead locally.

Microlite.name: Microlite
Microlite.description: Microlite gems are small yet very hard, suitable for cutting into faceted gems used to adorn swords, armor, shields, and horse tack. Beyond their decorative uses, they also serve as a practical substitute for traditional squid or octopus ink.

Mine Rails.name: Mine Rails
Mine Rails.description: Made out of steel to sustain the heavy loads from carts carrying ore and rocks. Blacksmiths, when crafting these, are required to use the most pristine materials they have in stock to ensure they stand the test of time and do not bend under extreme pressures and temperatures.

Mishanite.name: Mishanite
Mishanite.description: Mishanite is a uniquely sought-after gem, much like Harumite. Those who seek it can mold it into any desired shape or color, and multiple manifestations can coexist. Despite its lack of tangible value to others, Mishanite holds immense sentimental significance. Finding this gem evokes a profound sense of personal warmth and closeness.

Mistletoe.name: Mistletoe
Mistletoe.description: Mistletoe is culturally referred to as the kissing plant, for the way it grows as well as being associated with some winter holidays. Mistletoe represents romance, fertility, and vitality. They grow attached to their host tree or shrub through which they extract water and nutrients from the host plant. Used by herbalists everywhere, for blood conditions, such as arthritis, or for those who have conjugal issues in their homes.

Moleskin.name: Moleskin
Moleskin.description: A heavy cotton fabric which got its name due to the soft feeling to the touch, which bears similarity to mole's fur. The most common use of moleskin is for creating trousers, however, it is also usually a prime material for bandaging.

Monel.name: Monel
Monel.description: Monel is created by combining nickel and copper, and it has minimal iron content. It boasts exceptional corrosion resistance, making it suitable for a wide range of applications. Whether it's for dog tags, piping, metal sheets, or nails used in saltwater ships, objects made from this alloy typically exhibit extended durability.

Moonshine.name: Moonshine
Moonshine.description: Moonshine is a homemade, unaged strong liquor. It is speculated that moonshine can either give you perfect eyesight or cause you to lose it entirely. However, all records tend to be inaccurate as this information is gathered after the consumption of the drink.

Morganite.name: Morganite
Morganite.description: Morganite stands out for its multifaceted utility, harnessing a blend of arcane attributes reminiscent of both emerald and aquamarine. This distinctive combination makes it highly prized among gem enthusiasts.

Morion.name: Morion
Morion.description: Also known as Smoky Quartz. One of the rarer forms of quartz. Due to it being almost opaque, it does have some similar proprieties to the Rose Quartz. It's usually sought after by nefarious people as it is mainly used in necromancy. When bones are infused with Moriom they become as hard as steel.

Morning Breeze.name: 晨风 Morning Breeze
Morning Breeze.description: 这种无酒精饮品十分清爽。它主要由冷泡茶制成，并以柠檬和薄荷调味，再加入一小勺糖。若在清晨享用，尤其当阳光透过杯壁洒入其中时，它会泛起奇妙的金色光辉；人们也相信它能够治愈一切宿醉。

Mugwort.name: Mugwort
Mugwort.description: A common herb that is a favorite by herbalists everywhere. As a plant, it is easy to come-by as it grows in all biomes, and is used to resolve digestive problems, used for sedation, even as a laxative for the upset stomach.

Mulled Wine.description: Mulled wine becomes a staple drink as the colder seasons begin. The wine is boiled and then infused with sugar, black pepper, cinnamon, cloves, and orange peels. Served hot, it is rumored to cure the common cold, though it is primarily enjoyed for its warming effect.
Mulled Wine.name: Mulled Wine

Murder Cake.name: Murder Cake
Murder Cake.description: These I first encountered this cake at a masquerade in Sibiu, during Red Harvest, where the nobility have a taste for games that would curl the toes of a honest fisher. A Murder Cake is no simple dessert, it's the centerpiece of a theatrical evening where guests play at being detectives and killers. The cake itself is a grand, multi-tiered affair, often dark as a starless night with frosting the colour of old blood or fashioned to look like a gothic manor, but it also can be as simple as, a layer of cake with jam and a knife sticking out of it.

Mystery Soup.name: Mystery Soup
Mystery Soup.description: Similar to Meat of the Hunt, this soup is made from the leftovers of the day's hunt. It is mostly offered as a challenge for adventurers who claim they can eat anything without repercussions or as a hazing ritual for new patrons, providing amusement for the tavern keeper and other guests, as the new comers don't know better.

Nails.name: Nails
Nails.description: Nails are commonly made out of bronze or iron, and are mainly used as fasteners in construction, from small roofing nails to large iron spikes. They are also occasionally made out of copper or steel, particularly in environments where corrosion resistance is a key factor, such as coastal or riverside areas.

Natron.name: Natron
Natron.description: Natron is harvested directly as a salt mixture from dry salt lake beds, and it has multiple applications by both Alchemist and Herbalists. Herbalists mix Natron with herbs to treat minor wounds and cuts, while Alchemists use it as a binding agent for the more corrosive or explosive tinctures they create.

Nerium.name: Nerium
Nerium.description: Most commonly known as Oleander, it is a small shrub used for gardening ornamentation. When used by highly skilled herbalists, they can make sleeping tinctures and cure some types of skin irritations.

Nettle.name: Nettle
Nettle.description: Or Stinging Nettle, it is used mainly by practitioners of medicine to help treat muscle pains of the weary adventurers or the joints of the elderly. Experienced Alchemists use them in potions of healing as one of the base ingredients

Nickel Ingot.name: Nickel Ingot
Nickel Ingot.description: A nickel ingot is a small, perfectly formed bar of pure nickel. It is said to be incredibly dense and heavy, and can only be forged by the most skilled blacksmiths. The metal has a shiny, silver color that glimmers in the light, and is an incredibly resilient metal, able to withstand even the strongest blows. Many tales and legends surround this rare and valuable metal, with some claiming that it holds magical properties and can bestow objects with incredible strength and durability, however in most regions it’s main purpose is for the creation of multiple types of coinage.

Night Fangs.name: Night Fangs
Night Fangs.description: These fangs are a currency for all the night hunters, traded for specialized weaponry. The hunters hunt the creatures of the night, and after the hunt, even if they don't kill the captured creature, they remove their feeding fangs, rendering them harmless.

Nutmeg.name: Nutmeg
Nutmeg.description: Nutmeg is the seed or the ground spice derived from the seed, of several tree species. Nutmeg and Mace the red cover of the nutmeg seed, is used to flavor baked goods, meat, fish, and vegetables, and in preserving and pickling or for seasonal beverages such as eggnog, mulled cider, and mulled wine. Herbalists use Nutmeg as it may help relieve bloating, cramps, and upset stomachs due to food intolerance or allergies.

Oak Log.name: Oak Log
Oak Log.description: The logs are usually processed into lumber, however, it's also commonly used in bakeries, furnaces, or home fireplaces as kindle.

Obsidian.name: Obsidian
Obsidian.description: Lava when cooling, leaves behind huge lumps of Obsidian. This gem is used to make short-bladed weapons, tools, ornaments, kitchen wares, and mirrors. Some ill-reputed factions use a form of jagged edge rings created from obsidian to create small deep cuts into their intended targets.

Obsidian Ingot.name: Obsidian Ingot
Obsidian Ingot.description: An obsidian ingot is a small, expertly chiseled bar of pure obsidian. It is said to be incredibly dense and heavy, and can only be crafted by the most skilled blacksmiths. The metal has a deep, dark color that seems to absorb light, but when struck, it glows with a fiery inner light.

Ochre Fish.name: Ochre Fish
Ochre Fish.description: Ochre Fish is a transparent type of fish, somewhat acidic and sticky to the touch, known to corrode most hooks when captured. Usually found in seas and oceans that are not disturbed by ship traffic. Capturing this fish is associated with bad omens, for more experienced fishermen, as whole schools of the Ochre Fish have a tendency to stick themselves to the belly of fishing boats, slowly corroding the material and sinking them. Experiments have been conducted to refine its acidic secretion into potent solvents or even weapons by alchemists.

Oyster.name: Oyster
Oyster.description: They are usually harvested for food and for their rare pearls. Wizards on occasion use their shells as magical components, however, the spell it is used for has been kept a secret. In times long past, there was word that rare green emerald oysters had existed as well, however, no one has seen one lately. Some Taverns offer Oysters as a delicacy depending on the region.

Painite.name: Painite
Painite.description: Painite is the rarest gem in the realms. Nobody has a specific price for it and some say it can buy entire kingdoms, while others say it has no value. So far, besides the ornamentation of jewelry pieces, no other uses have been found. Researchers do study these specific gems for their more arcane applications; however, no discoveries have been made as of yet.

Palo Verde.name: Palo Verde
Palo Verde.description: The Palo Verde tree can be easily recognized by its green-colored bark. This tree is typically harvested for its bark, which can be used for multiple purposes. The inner bark can be woven into rope or baskets, or used for making chair bottoms. Additionally, it is utilized for making canoes, and clothing, and as a surface for paintings and maps. The bark can also be used as shingles and wall coverings.

Pancakes.name: Pancakes
Pancakes.description: Eggs, milk, sugar, and flour are all it takes to make delicious pancakes. When topped with syrup or fruit, they become one of the most satisfying and sweet breakfasts offered by taverns.

Pandean Pipe.name: Pandean Pipe
Pandean Pipe.description: The Pandean pipe, often referred to as the pan flute or panpipe, is made from closed tubes of increasing lengths. Sound is produced when air is blown in its tubes, causing them to resonate and produce a unique sound. This instrument is widely used in music and traditional performances.

Paring Chisel.name: Paring Chisel
Paring Chisel.description: Paring chisels, favored by most carpenters, are designed for delicately shaving thin slivers of wood. Their primary use is in carefully shaving off thin amounts of wood when fitting joints and the long length of these chisels provides carpenters with maximum control.

Peach.name: Peach
Peach.description: Peaches are widely eaten fresh and are also baked in pies and cobblers. For the winter they are stored in water with sugar and honey or provided as refreshments for the warmer seasons.

Peach Figs.name: Peach Figs
Peach Figs.description: Called also the flat peach or doughnut peach, very similar to the sister fruit, but oblate in shape. They tend to be a bit rarer as only very few regions cultivate these, however, compared to their regular counterpart, the peach, they are way sweeter.

Peach Pits.name: Peach Pits
Peach Pits.description: Peach trees are notoriously finicky when it comes to planting, demanding patience and knowledge. Their seeds need to be kept in cold storage for an extended period before they’re ready for planting. This period of dormancy ensures that when the time comes to plant, the seed will sprout and grow into a healthy tree. Even a small mistake in timing or storage can result in failure, which is why only the most experienced farmhands and herbalists take on the responsibility of planting the pits.

Peanut.name: Peanut
Peanut.description: Contrary to popular belief, peanuts don't grow on trees, they are cultivated as potatoes or onions. Boiled or roasted peanuts are a very popular snack. At mills, the peanut can be also turned into peanut oil or flour which are commonly used in cooking.

Pennyroyal.name: Pennyroyal
Pennyroyal.description: Herbalists are on a high lookout for the Pennyroyal plant, when ground down into oils it is used for the trusty four, or two-legged companions as a flea replant. Its also usually used in soaps and fragrances for perfumes as it smells very similar to spearmint when crushed.

Peridot.name: Peridot
Peridot.description: Peridot is a popular and affordable gemstone and is faceted into many cuts, and used in all forms of jewelry, especially rings, earrings, necklaces, and bracelets. Magic can be used to build different structures, however, depending on the size of the building, a vast amount of the gemstone might be required.

Permits.name: Permits
Permits.description: Usually not officially named as permits, they go by various titles such as writs, charters, licenses, or letters patent. Examples include a License to Bear Arms, which allows individuals to carry weapons in the presence of nobility, or a Permit to Travel or Safe Conduct license, often granted to envoys tasked with delivering messages or goods. Another example is a Resource Extraction license, which grants rights to harvest timber from a designated area of a noble’s forest. Ore, meat, and other resources also fall under the same type of license, depending on the material being gathered.

Pietra Serena.name: Pietra Serena
Pietra Serena.description: Pietra Serena is a refined marble variety, known for its characteristic blue-gray tones. Other shades, including deep blue and black, also exist. Highly valued by artists, it is commonly used in sculptures and architectural embellishments such as pilasters, entablatures, and decorative elements.

Pill Woodlouse.name: Pill Woodlouse
Pill Woodlouse.description: It goes by many names, including common pill-bug, slaters, common pill woodlouse, roly-poly, potato bug, doodle bug, and carpenter woodlouse. Some farmers keep them after a successful harvest to clean the soil, as they feed on dead plants and prevent the spread of weeds.

Pine Log.name: Pine Log
Pine Log.description: One of the most harvested types of wood in woodland areas. They are good for making cabin logs, fencing, or signs.

Pine Plank.name: Pine Plank
Pine Plank.description: The lumber is a light color, and it's mainly used in construction since it's quite flexible and resilient. It can be used for window and door frames, rails, and construction mold.

Pineapple.name: Pineapple
Pineapple.description: In some regions, the pineapple is considered a significant cultural icon of luxury, as its rarity created a surreal demand with the noble folk. In most cuisines around the realms, it is mainly served as a fruit or as a juice. It is also a common garnish for hams and meats. Chunks of fruit are used in desserts such as fruit salads, cakes, sweets, and baked goods. A form of vinegar can also be created from it after it is combined with grain vinegar.

Pink Salmon.name: Pink Salmon
Pink Salmon.description: Pink Salmon is often used in salted and smoked preparations, and even sushi. Its mild taste pairs well with citrus glazes, herb butter, or even spicy rubs, making it an amazing choice in the kitchen. Because of its softer texture compared to other salmon varieties, it is often preferred in soups and chowders as well.

Pink Waxcaps.name: Pink Waxcaps
Pink Waxcaps.description: Pink wax caps are also referred to as "ballerinas" because the pink cap looks like a ballerina's tutu. The mushrooms are eatable and have a unique taste, that does resemble a fine sour cream. Primarily used by chefs for garnishing meals as they are quite rare. Herbalists tend to use them as a ground paste, to ward off any magical diseases.

Pistachio.name: Pistachio
Pistachio.description: Chopped pistachios are commonly mixed into or sprinkled atop desserts like cream, cake, truffles, and pastries. With a bit of dough they can be folded into quick breads, muffins, cupcakes, or yeast breads, they contribute crunch and buttery flavor.

Pollucite.name: Pollucite
Pollucite.description: Pollucite is usually a transparent, brittle white stone. In certain regions, it is crushed and mixed with wine for consumption, believed to bring exceptional good fortune. Despite traditional beliefs, healers and herbalists warn that it often causes severe stomach pain.

Popcorn Kennels.name: Popcorn Kennels
Popcorn Kennels.description: Popcorn is a variety of corn kernels that expands and puffs up when heated. The term also refers to the snack food made by heating these kernels. They can be topped with sugar, salt, butter, caramel, or cheese.

Poplar Lumber.name: Poplar Lumber
Poplar Lumber.description: It is perfect for items like pallets, boxes, and crates, but also, artisan bowls, wooden plates, and other fine carpentry. Due to its relatively modest price point, poplar is a great choice for commercial uses. However, it can dent or scratch easily, making it undesirable for high-wear items, as well as, over time its color fades to a medium brown within months.

Pocketwatch.name: Pocketwatch
Pocketwatch.description: The pocket watches come in two different types. A hunter-case pocket watch is a case with a spring-hinged circular metal lid or cover, that closes over the watch-dial, protecting them from dust, scratches and other damage or debris while an open-faced is one in which the case lacks a metal cover to protect the front glass.

Portobello Mushroom.name: Portobello Mushroom
Portobello Mushroom.description: Portobello mushrooms are widely cultivated and prized for their versatility in salads and as toppings for various dishes.

Potato.name: Potato
Potato.description: One of the most cultivated vegetables throughout the realms, making it one of the most famous ones as well. They can be served as a cooked vegetable, in whole or mashed form, and are also used as pie filling. In some colder regions, it is widely believed that potatoes are used to create very strong alcohol.

Pumpkin Stew.description: The recipe for Pumpkin Stew varies depending on the region or, in some rare cases, among taverns. Multiple recipes exist containing either a variety of meats or solely vegetables, offering a wide range of culinary experiences.
Pumpkin Stew.name: Pumpkin Stew

Pure Day.name: 纯昼 Pure Day
Pure Day.description: 纯昼通常只是普通平民在热浪中继续饮酒的蹩脚借口。这种酒本身并不烈，味道类似清凉的柠檬水，但基底却是麦酒。有一则经久不衰的传闻声称，若将其与晨风和永夜混合，便能获得不朽。但考虑到配方各有差异，这听起来完全就是编造出来的谎言。

Pure-Metal Brass Ingot.name: Pure-Metal Brass Ingot
Pure-Metal Brass Ingot.description: Pure-Metal Brass Ingots are manufactured from the finest curated copper and zinc. They have become increasingly popular for crafting religious objects such as candlesticks, cathedral bells, and reliquaries, often fetching a high price. Though slightly softer than bronze, its corrosion resistance makes it ideal for naval and coastal and naval military gear.

Pure-Metal Bronze Ingot.name: Pure-Metal Bronze Ingot
Pure-Metal Bronze Ingot.description: Pure-Metal Bronze Ingots are made from an alloy of the most refined chunks of copper and tin, in equal amounts. Skilled blacksmiths at prestigious smelters refine the metals through precise smelting techniques, ensuring that every ingot maintains a uniform composition. Without impurities or flaws, these blacksmiths can forge higher than normal quality tools, weapons, armor, and building materials such as decorative tiles for flooring or detailed sculptures.

Purple Berries.name: Purple Berries
Purple Berries.description: Purple berries are palpably sweet, Gatherers and Herbalists recommend eating them in small quantities, as health complications may occur. Brewmasters use them to give their ales more interesting colors, as well as make their beverages sweeter.

Purple Brittlegill.name: Purple Brittlegill
Purple Brittlegill.description: The Purple Brittlegill is large, with a dark vinaceous or purple cap, and white or cream-colored gills, a mushroom that is edible. Herbalists recommend that due to some brittlegills being toxic, it's important to only eat mushrooms that you can recognize. If unsure, better avoid it.

Purple Emperor.name: Purple Emperor
Purple Emperor.description: Purple Emperor is an magnificent butterfly which flies high in the treetops of very dense forests. Alchemists, usually keep a few in a butterfly house for breeding, as they are an rare reagent in some of the most rare potions.

Purple Pink Diamond.name: Purple Pink Diamond
Purple Pink Diamond.description: Sometimes also referred to as fancy diamonds, they are extremely rare. It is also speculated that the wearers of specific diamonds can be protected from some spells or enchantments.

Purple Vampire Crab.name: Purple Vampire Crab
Purple Vampire Crab.description: A an amazing thing I have heard, from an old delta crabber, is that the intensity of the purple on their shells predicts the severity of the coming winter. The deeper the purple, the harder the frosts. He also swore that if you keep one in a tank, it will only thrive if you add a pinch of silt from its home delta each month. He called it "homesickness," and I think he might be right.

Puzzle Box.name: Puzzle Box
Puzzle Box.description: This small toy comes with a disclaimer stating that it contains small parts. While it is mechanized, the Puzzle Box is mostly aimed at young children to help them with cognitive development. It contains many small cogs and gears. However, when it is finally opened, parents always leave a treat for the children.

Pyrope.name: Pyrope
Pyrope.description: Pyrope, a type of garnet known for its exquisite beauty, also holds military significance. It is believed that adorning weaponry with this garnet allows it to penetrate the thickest hides or demolish the strongest doors, aided by arcane forces.

Quartz.name: Quartz
Quartz.description: The deep sky blue color of this gem, somewhat rare, doesn't have many uses and it's used for ornamentation. When spells require gems that are not attached to a value, Quartz is the go-to gemstone for casters, as it has low price.

Rabbit.name: Rabbit
Rabbit.description: Commonly referred to as the meat rabbit, these rabbits are primarily harvested for their meat. However, in regions where hunting was not readily available, both wild rabbits and domestic rabbits were also harvested for pelts.

Radish.name: Radish
Radish.description: The root of the radish is usually eaten raw, although it is more often than not steamed. They are mostly used in salads but they also appear in some regional dishes. The seeds of radishes can be pressed to extract radish seed oil.

Rake.name: Rake
Rake.description: Rakes come in various types, but the two most common are garden rakes and soil rakes. Garden rakes are effectively designed to pick up leaves and other garden debris without causing damage to the lawn and the soil beneath. On the other hand, soil rakes are intended for leveling and grading the soil before plants and seeds are planted.

Ramen.name: Ramen
Ramen.description: Ramen is a flavorful soup made with noodles and pork, typically seasoned with soy sauce and miso. It is often served with a variety of toppings, including sliced bacon, scallions, and sometimes fish, dried seaweed, or eggs.

Ramie.name: Ramie
Ramie.description: Ramie, also called grass linen, is a fiber derived from a type of nettle. Known for its strength and luster, it is an ideal material for creating fabrics suited to warm climates. Beyond textiles, it is also used in making fishing nets, filter cloths, and occasionally, paper.

Raspberry.name: Raspberry
Raspberry.description: Raspberries are grown for the fresh fruit market, and most of them end up made either in a variety of juices or as a fruit puree. They are also sought after by bakers as the raspberry pie is one of the most favorite sweets of the young folk.

Red Paper Wasp.name: Red Paper Wasp
Red Paper Wasp.description: Red Paper is a non-hostile type of wasp that becomes aggressive only when its nest is threatened. Their venom can be collected by herbalists to produce medicine, safely harvested due to the insect’s docile behavior.

Red Revenant's Refresher.name: Red Revenant's Refresher
Red Revenant's Refresher.description: The base recipe for this unique concoction is almost identical to the traditional Kompot recipe, with one major ingredient change that sets it apart. Instead of water, the sanguine fluid of creatures is used as a replacement. This is to provide some variation in taste for the night beings. The sanguine fluid is harvested from various creatures, ranging from small rodents to large beasts, depending on the desired flavor. However, due to the taboo nature of its primary ingredient, it is often created and consumed in secrecy and with caution.

Retama.name: Retama
Retama.description: Retama is usually a tree that is not harvested for its wood, however, the leaves and the seed pods make excellent food for most grazing animals. Herbalists make from the leaves and young branches of the Retama tree teas to treat pain, fevers, or infections.

Rosaline.name: Rosaline
Rosaline.description: Rosaline, also referred to as pink zoisite, is a gemstone known for its ability to exhibit three distinct colors, purple, red, and either blue or green, depending on the viewing angle. When crushed, Rosaline is used to create hair or paint dye, producing a lovely pink-red hue.

Royal Signet.name: Royal Signet
Royal Signet.description: Royal Signet is a piece of jewelry, typically custom-made for the nobility. The most renowned jewelers are usually commissioned for this precise work, as the impression it leaves in wax must be as detailed and clear as possible. The band is generally crafted from gold, while the signet itself is forged from a combination of alloys that can be replaced or reworked into the ring with ease if needed.

Rubellite.name: Rubellite
Rubellite.description: Rubellite, a pink variant of tourmaline, stands out as the rarest gem in its family. Occasionally mistaken for ruby, it is a target for exploitation by unscrupulous merchants.

Ruby Spinel.name: Ruby Spinel
Ruby Spinel.description:
"They say, if you ingest these, although, I wouldn't recommend it one bit, you can remember spells. Now I wonder, how can someone forget?" 

Spinel is a rare mineral, and due to it, used to substitute even rarer ones like rubies. The blue variant is one of the most expensive gemstones on the market.

Rutile Powder.name: Rutile Powder
Rutile Powder.description: Rutile when ground into a fine powder, it turns into a beautiful pigment, bringing color to canvases and ceramics alike. In the world of medicine, its strength and resistance to corrosion make it a primary material, protecting implants and ensuring their longevity.

Rutiled Quartz.name: Rutiled Quartz
Rutiled Quartz.description: Rutilated quartz is a variety of quartz that contains needle-like strikes within the gem. These inclusions may be distributed randomly or in bundles, which sometimes are arranged star-like, and they can be sparse or dense enough to make the quartz body nearly opaque. While in general inclusions often reduce the value of any other crystal, rutilated quartz is valued for the quality of these inclusions.

Sandalwood Lumber.name: Sandalwood Lumber
Sandalwood Lumber.description: Sandalwood is extremely rare and precious, it is used for intricate carvings, to make sturdy furniture, medicines, cosmetics, and religious rites. The heartwood of the tree is treasured for its aroma and is one of the finest natural materials for making beautifully carved furniture and tools.

Sanguine Cocktail.name: Sanguine Cocktail
Sanguine Cocktail.description: I first saw this in The Gilded Sea Horse, a tavern tucked away in a hidden back alley close to the docks, where everything smells of rot and old salt. Matel, the tavern owner, pushed the glass across the counter with the largest smirk her face could muster. In the guttering light it looks like fresh blood, a deep, viscous red that clings to the glass and runs slow enough to make you anxious. My heart skipped, then my tongue said other things. It’s sweet at first, like honeyed brambles warmed in your palm, then a pinch of lemon snaps through so you wake up. Underneath it all you get the clean bite of a highland gin distilled up in the cold hills behind Iteshti.

Sanidine.name: Sanidine
Sanidine.description: Sanidine is a gemstone commonly found on mountaintops and sporadically in desert areas. Nomadic tribes of the desert believe that placing it in water assists in purification, although definitive evidence is absent without arcane assistance.

Sausages.description: Created from a variety of meats, vegetables, and spices such as beef, pork, or lamb, with ingredients like peppercorns, mustard seed, coriander, paprika, chili powder, bay leaves, and more. Sausages can also be dried or smoked for a longer shelf life and a wide range of varieties of flavors.
Sausages.name: Sausages

Scarecrow.name: Scarecrow
Scarecrow.description: The scarecrow, rooted in popular tradition, is a mannequin typically stuffed with straw. Its primary purpose is to deter birds or other animals from consuming seeds, crops, and fruit.

Scorpion.name: Scorpion
Scorpion.description: Scorpions are nocturnal hunters that can be found mainly in dry regions, hiding under rocks and within burrows during the day. Their iconic tail carries a venomous stinger used to subdue prey and defend against threats. While their sting is painful to common folk, it is rarely fatal, and alchemists seek their venom for use in various potent medicine concoctions.

Screamsickle Shake.name: Screamsickle Shake
Screamsickle Shake.description: A small superstition clings to the Screamsicle Shake, young children are told that if you spill a drop on the wooden floor, will make next year crop fail. I have a feeling this was invented by tired mothers who didn’t want sticky footprints on their clean floors. Still, I never dared spill my own cup at my mother's house, and I’m not about to test the spirits patience now.

Seafinder Coin.name: Seafinder Coin
Seafinder Coin.description: A coin made for sea captains, for long trips, mainly used for superstition. Prior to setting sail on a lenghty journey, the captain will throw one of these coins into the waters to appease the gods or the sea itself depending on his beliefs. The coin is usually made out of silver with an gem that can range from Blue Topaz to the inexpensive Chrysocolla.

Seed Potato.name: Seed Potato
Seed Potato.description: Seed potatoes are potatoes that are chosen for planting in the next growing cycle. Unlike mature potatoes that have been through several harvests, seed potatoes are harvested earlier in the growing process. While they are used for planting, they are still suitable for cooking just like regular potatoes.

Seeds - Corn.name: Seeds - Corn
Seeds - Corn.description: Corn seeds are small, hard kernels that come from the corn plant. They are used to grow corn, which is a staple food in many parts of the world, as well as used to create biscuits, fillings, cookies, beer, and ales. Making it a highly demanded trade good.

Seeds - Hyacinth.name: Seeds - Hyacinth
Seeds - Hyacinth.description: The small, round bulbs of the hyacinth plant were traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, such as skin rashes, as well as in cooking in a variety of dishes. However, they are mainly sold for indoor decoration by planting them in flowerpots.

Seeds - Sunflower.name: Seeds - Sunflower
Seeds - Sunflower.description: Sunflower seeds are small, hard seeds that come from the sunflower plant. They are often roasted and eaten as a snack or used to produce oil and other products.

Seeds - Wheat.name: Seeds - Wheat
Seeds - Wheat.description: Wheat seeds are small, brown seeds that come from the wheat plant. They are used to grow wheat, a grain that, when ground, is used to make bread, pasta, and other baked goods. It is also one of the most traded goods, along with corn seeds.

Shapeshifter.name: Shapeshifter
Shapeshifter.description: Traders like me see jars of apple jam moving by the crate in early autumn, traded from orchard stewards to dairy farms and back again, all so this one concoction can be made for a handful of nights. It doesn’t hold very well, by following morning it clots and sours, which is why no one ships it far, one of it's reasons that it got its weird name, but kids love it.

Ship Log.name: Ship Log
Ship Log.description: A detailed log written by the captain of a vessel that contains information categorized by date. It includes the captain's thoughts at the time, as well as the locations traveled, ship status and repairs, star charts, onboard incidents, rations remaining, direction headed, and cargo. Most captains primarily use it as a journal for their current voyage.

Shrimp.name: Shrimp
Shrimp.description: Shrimp are usually captured through trawling, a fishing method in which one or more ships drag a large net behind them, sweeping up any sea creatures in its path. Because the Shrimp is not the sole target of this fishing method, their availability can be quite inconsistent, leading to high prices in town markets.

Sickle.name: Sickle
Sickle.description: The sickle being one of the most common hand tools, finds extensive use in harvesting crops, cutting grass, and assisting in the gathering of vegetables from fields. The versatility of its blade extends to having a major role as a weapon in certain regions.

Siglos.name: Siglos
Siglos.description: These coins are crafted exclusively by monasteries for their parishioners who are merchants. They are typically made from stone and decorated with religious symbols and colors. The coins are distributed to merchants who travel to wealthier regions so that they can access services without having to pay extra. These services may include lodging, education, or meetings with the local high priests.

Silk.name: Silk
Silk.description: It is one of the rarest materials to find. It is mainly produced by either fantastical beasts or insects of different sizes. It is speculated that only the most trained gatherers can create something called "Pure Silk", however, some of them end up losing their lives sometimes, getting more than they bargained for from some eight-legged monsters.

Silphium.name: Silphium
Silphium.description: Silphium, also known as laserwort, is a rare and exotic plant utilized by expert alchemists and herbalists for various purposes. Its uses include functioning as a spice, a component in perfumes, an aphrodisiac, and a medicinal remedy. It treats ailments such as coughs, sore throats, fevers, indigestion, general pain, and warts, and it also acts as a contraceptive.

Siltstone Slabs.name: Siltstone Slabs
Siltstone Slabs.description: Siltstone blocks are primarily used as dimension stones, where they can be cut into blocks or slabs for architectural purposes or for polishing certain metals, such as steel. In high-density areas, they are commonly used for indoor flooring, landscape decoration, and garden walls.

Silver Earings.name: Silver Earings
Silver Earings.description: To avoid the high prices of gem-inlaid implements, earrings made solely from silver are also crafted. Aside from being a precious metal, silver offers the added benefit of being easier to mold, allowing artisans to create intricate patterns and detailed designs, while keeping the cost accessible to the average commoner.

Silver Goblet.name: Silver Goblet
Silver Goblet.description: Silver goblets, along with other pieces such as plates, platters, cutlery, and eating utensils, are ever-present in a noble household. These items are often commissioned in bulk, typically from a dedicated smithy or the local blacksmith.
